title: A Hierarchical Carbon Emission Factor Correction Method Considering Non-Grid-Connected Electricity
authors: Dawei Ma, Haotian Zhang, Ming Kong, Chao Ji, Wei Tang, Yang Su, Bin Xu, Chao Liu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Accurate carbon emission factors (CEFs) are essential for tracking and reducing emissions in power systems. Traditional average CEF methods suffer from coarse granularity, while carbon flow tracing approaches are computationally intensive and unsuitable for large-scale grids. Moreover, both ignore the environmental benefits of non-grid-connected electricity (NGCE), such as local rooftop PV or self-consumed distributed generation. This paper proposes a hierarchical correction method for regional CEFs that accounts for NGCE. Based on electrical partitioning theory, the grid is divided into three layers: provincial, prefectural, and end-user. A recursive calculation strategy is adopted to compute CEFs for grid-connected electricity, while NGCE is attributed to local nodes for final correction. Case studies validate the method’s feasibility, showing improved spatiotemporal resolution and accurate reflection of distributed generation benefits. The approach offers a practical pathway for carbon accounting in regional power systems.

title: System-Level Coordinated Control Model of Energy Storage Systems for Grid Security and Stability Analysis
authors: Yuhan Liu, Hanyang Dai, Xiong Xiao, Xiangtao Xiao, Xinli Song, Shaohang Hao, Yikang Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the large-scale integration of renewable energy, energy storage systems are playing an increasingly critical role in enhancing grid security and operational flexibility. This paper presents a system-level coordinated control model for energy storage systems participating in grid security and stability control, suitable for large-scale power system simulation. The study first analyzes the functions of energy storage systems in power flow control, frequency regulation, and voltage support. Based on this, a coordinated control framework is developed, integrating automatic generation control, automatic voltage control, security and stability control systems, and a priority-based weight allocation mechanism that coordinates command priorities with resource availability. The proposed model is validated through a four-machine two-area system simulation for secondary frequency regulation and a cascading fault case study based on an actual provincial power grid. Results demonstrate that the model accurately captures the multi-timescale dynamic characteristics of utility-scale energy storage systems and effectively supports grid security and stability analysis.

title: Photoacoustic signal from hemoglobin target
authors: Isabel Nunes Ramalho da Rocha, Juliana Oliveira da Silva Fernandes, Raymundo Cordero Garcia, Krishynan Santhy Fernandes Meirelles Araujo, Beatriz Ferreira de Carvalho Patricio, Ana Beatriz Furlanetto Pacheco, João Carlos Machado
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Photoacoustic (PA) systems have been extensively studied for their potential to obtain functional information from biological organs and tissues. Studies report the potential of endogenous molecules, such as hemoglobin (Hb), to generate photoacoustic signals. This study presents an experimental evaluation of a PA system developed at the Ultrasound Laboratory of the Biomedical Engineering Program at COPPE/UFRJ. For this purpose, a rectangular phantom was constructed from a 2% agarose solution using a mold produced by 3D printing, which had internal channels molded by catheters, which were removed after the agarose solidified. Initially, graphite was analyzed, used as a positive control for optical absorption, followed by a solution of human Hb at a concentration of 1 mg/ml. Photoacoustic excitation was performed using a DPSS laser source operating at 1064 nm, and the acoustic signals generated were detected by an ultrasonic transducer. The results demonstrated the acquisition of clearly detectable photoacoustic signals for both absorbents, highlighting the sensitivity of the system to Hb detection, as found in the literature. This study validates the proposed experimental methodology and establishes a basis for future studies.

title: Tyndall-Effect-Based Optical Monitoring of Micro-Plastic Pollution in Aquatic Environments
authors: Victor Ababii, Viorica Sudacevschi, Galina Marusic, Viorel Carbune
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: The paper presents the development and validation of an experimental system for monitoring microplastic pollution in aquatic environments, based on the Tyndall effect and artificial intelligence techniques. The proposed system uses a laser light source and a video acquisition module to detect light scattered by microplastic particles, with data processed in real time on the NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano Developer Kit (SBC) platform. Mathematical models and discrete algorithms are developed for correlating the optical signal with the concentration of microplastics, and the performance of the method is improved by integrating artificial neural networks such as CNN and LSTM. The experimental results, presented in the form of images and quantitative graphs, demonstrate the system's ability to identify the presence of microplastics and dynamically monitor concentration variations. The proposed solution offers a fast, non-invasive, and scalable approach with high potential for continuous monitoring of the aquatic environment.

title: Optimization Strategy for Micro Energy Grid Transactions in Multi-Level Energy Markets Based on Asymmetric Nash Bargaining
authors: Shan Liu, Kouyu Sun, Yiheng Liang, Kangyang Lv, Chenhui Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the advancement of the multi-level energy market, micro energy grid (MEG) operators are confronted with core issues such as insufficient transaction synergy and imbalance in the distribution of benefits among entities. This paper proposes a coordinated multi-level electricity market trading strategy and constructs an integrated multi-level energy trading framework covering the energy wholesale market, the peer-to-peer electricity market, and the shared energy storage leasing market. To balance efficiency and fairness in multi-level energy market coordination, an optimization method based on the asymmetric Nash bargaining theory is further developed. First, the strategy quantifies the actual contribution of each participating entity in multi-level transactions, and scientifically sets differentiated bargaining weights for all entities. On this basis, it realizes the global optimization of the overall operating cost of the MEG system while taking into account the benefit demands of each trading entity, thus achieving a reasonable and fair distribution of benefits. Finally, the alternating direction method of multipliers is adopted to solve the proposed optimization model, and case studies based on actual scenarios are conducted to verify the strategy. The results show that the proposed strategy can effectively improve the market coordination efficiency of the multi-level energy market, significantly reduce the comprehensive operating cost of the entire MEG system, and achieve a fair and reasonable distribution of trading benefits for each participating entity, which fully verifies the feasibility and effectiveness of the strategy.

title: Maintaining Lot Schedule Adherence through Overlapping Q-times via Admission Control
authors: Thomas Hewlett, Srikanth Ramakrishnan, Himanshu Mogra, Shiladitya Chakravorty, Detlef Pabst
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Dispatching into a time-constrained sequence (Q-time or Time-link zone) poses a unique challenge for high volume manufacturing (HVM) factories, as schedule priority is, instead, often trumped by the protection of wafer quality. As fab processes mature, these constrained sequences may also continue to increase in complexity. This paper outlines a method by which both objectives of quality and schedule adherence can be achieved in an automated manner.

title: Unload Clean Removal for Showerhead Pattern Defects Improvement
authors: Hong Kai Tan, Kenneth Chih Yuang Mah, Ker Peng Lau, Edward See, Chek Chou Chiam
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: In CVD systems, clean recipe settings are closely related to the three core tenets of manufacturing, which are cost, productivity, and quality. In the interest of throughput, a cleaning configuration termed as "Clean on Unload Loadlock" may be utilized to process multiple wafers consecutively before the cleaning process is triggered to maximize output, which is synchronized to the wafer loading and unloading cycle. In this paper, we show that this cleaning configuration introduces a critical process imbalance. Specifically, in twin-reactor CVD systems, one chamber becomes repeatedly exposed to cyclic cleaning erosion, due to final wafers of a lot processing only in one chamber, leading to the partner chamber being uncoated during the subsequent cleaning and subjected to chemical attack by fluorine radicals. Hence, the removal of such cleaning configuration is proposed to mitigate showerhead pattern defects, with an improvement from an average of 4.6 cases per year to zero cases since implementation to date, and gas cost savings.

title: A Data-driven Approach to Synergistic Resilient Assessment and Investment Optimization for Sustainable Operation of Island Power Grids
authors: Yuetong Li, Haoyang Yu, Hongfan Li, Xueyong Ding, Shifeng Wang, Liansheng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Island power grids face the core challenge of improving power supply reliability while controlling investment costs. To scientifically quantify the trade-off between these two factors and seek the optimal solution for social welfare, this paper constructs a data-driven resilience-economy synergistic assessment and investment decision optimization framework. Firstly, comprehensive system resilience metrics, total social costs, and marginal resilience costs are defined. Subsequently, a three-part methodology comprising power outage loss prediction, core metric calculation, and visualized decision-making is proposed. Finally, taking the Hainan power grid as a case study, five investment scenarios and three disaster severity levels are designed. The research demonstrates that there exists a clear optimal economic equilibrium point for grid resilience investment, with distinct patterns in marginal cost variations. Optimizing investment strategies can effectively enhance social welfare. This study provides a scientific quantitative analysis tool for refined grid planning and investment decision-making.

title: Electronic Fratricide in Air Defence Operations While Countering Uas in Tactical Battle Areas
authors: M Y Khan, Pranjal Agarwal, Rajagopal Chellamani Srikanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: Electronic fratricide in air defence operations refers to the unintended interference, degradation or neutralisation of friendly sensors, communication networks, navigation aids and weapon control systems due to uncoordinated electronic emissions within a contested electromagnetic environment. The rapid proliferation of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS), operating across diverse frequency bands with low radar crosssections, has compelled air defence forces to increasingly rely on electronic countermeasures, jammers, spoofers and directed energy weapons[1] [2]. While these measures are essential for countering hostile UAS, inadequate spectrum coordination and decentralised control can inadvertently disrupt friendly systems, resulting in loss of situational awareness, delayed engagements and increased risk of blue-on-blue incidents[2] [4]. This paper analyses the evolution of the UAS threat, its employment in contemporary conflicts, existing counter-UAS measures and the primary causes of electronic fratricide. Case studies from recent conflicts are examined to highlight operational risks, followed by recommendations to mitigate electronic fratricide through centralised command and control, integrated spectrum management, training and procedural reforms.

title: Analysis and Prediction of Influencing Factors of Power Carbon Emissions in Guizhou Province Based on LMDI Decomposition and Multi-NAR Integrated Nonlinear Regression
authors: Chen Luo, Julong Chen, Xuepeng Mou, Bin Wang, Shiping Yang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Understanding the drivers of power carbon emissions is essential for improving emission forecasting and supporting low-carbon energy planning. Taking Guizhou Province as a case study, this study analyzes and predicts power sector carbon emissions during 2007–2022. The logarithmic mean Divisia index method is applied to decompose emission changes into multiple driving effects. The results indicate that urban per capita GDP, electricity intensity, and population scale are the dominant drivers. The GDP effect accounts for about 59% of the emission growth, while improvements in electricity intensity offset nearly 47% of the increase. Based on these factors, NAR, NARX, and FNN models are employed for forecasting analysis. Rolling test results show prediction errors ranging from 12.89% to 15.31% MAPE, providing a reliable evaluation of carbon emission trends in Guizhou.

title: Study on Si-contamination in Developable BARC (DBARC) in High-K Metal Gate Process
authors: Wu Wenbin, Zhang Lei, Li Xiaojing, Fu Yong Feng
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a study on the effect of a Si-containing DBARC chemical during lithography step in HKMG process. Compared to normal good batch and unopen bottle of same type of DBARC chemical, an additional thin film was observed if the Si-containing DBARC material was coated together with photoresist. The formation of such Si–O film on top of wafer surface later caused related device failure. Cross-section TEM and EDX mapping techniques were used to characterize the film. Further experiment of characterization of this thin film with SIMS analysis indicates a potential application of in-line SIMS tool to monitor contamination from DBARC.

title: Intelligent Modeling for Deriving Human Lens Stiffness Based on Patients’ Demographic Features with Outcome in Phacoemulsification Surgery
authors: Sara Rahmati, Hadi Tabesh
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Background: Accurate preoperative assessment of human lens stiffness is a critical determinant in modern cataract surgery, particularly for optimizing ultrasound energy in phacoemulsification. Current clinical grading methods often rely on subjective judgment, which limits personalized surgical planning. Methods: We developed a predictive framework utilizing routine demographic and clinical features to estimate the gold-standard ex vivo elastic modulus (Young's modulus) of 50 cataractous human lenses, obtained via extracapsular cataract extraction (ECCE). A suite of supervised regression algorithms, including Support Vector Regression (SVR), was implemented and rigorously compared based on Mean Squared Error (MSE) and R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> metrics. Results: SVR achieved the highest predictive performance on the test set (R<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>=0.9409). Feature importance analysis consistently identified cataract grade (LOCS III), lens diameter, and age as the most influential factors driving stiffness prediction. Conclusion: This study establishes a successful intelligent proof-of-concept demonstrating the feasibility of correlating readily available clinical data with fundamental biomechanical properties using advanced machine learning. The models lay the necessary groundwork for developing non-invasive, AI-driven diagnostic tools to enhance surgical safety and efficiency.

title: Quantum Enhanced Data-driven Learning for Defect Reduction in Etching Process
authors: Zusing Yang, Li-Wei Wang, Yi-Che Chen, Yi-Han Chao, Cheng-En Tsai, Ming-Tsung Wu, Hong-Ji Lee, Nan-Tzu Lian, Tahone Yang, Kuang-Chao Chen, Chih-Yuan Lu
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Defect reduction in plasma etching is a critical challenge in semiconductor manufacturing, traditionally addressed through empirical tuning of multiple process parameters with significant resource and time consumption. This study presents a quantum-enhanced data-driven learning framework that integrates quantum neural networks (QNN), quantum annealing (QA), and quantum evolution (QE) to optimize 25 etching parameters simultaneously for improved defect mitigation and profile control. The quantum circuits and annealing procedures were simulated using PennyLane’s variational quantum simulator backend. The proposed approach enables extrapolative prediction beyond historical domain knowledge, overcoming limitations of conventional machine learning methods such as artificial neural networks (ANNs). Experimental results demonstrate a substantial reduction in total defect density from 16.39 to 0.42 defects/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> while maintaining stable etching critical dimension (ECD) control. Notably, QE uncovers hidden sensitivities in parameters previously held constant, highlighting the potential of quantum-enhanced learning to both optimize processes and reveal latent manufacturing insights. This work underscores the promise of hybrid quantum-classical machine learning frameworks as the means for systematic process optimization in advanced semiconductor fabrication.

title: Controller Tuning for First- to Fourth-Order Inertia Object Models with Astatic Behavior Using the Polynomial Method
authors: Bartolomeu Izvoreanu, Adrian Secrieru, Nadejda Palamarciuc, Ion Fiodorov, Dumitru Moraru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: This paper describes the procedure for tuning a controller for models of control objects with first- to fourth-order inertia and astatic behavior using the polynomial method, with imposed values of the damping ratio and settling time of the synthesized system. The numerator and denominator polynomials of the transfer function of the object model are factorized into factors with zeros located in the left-hand and right-hand parts of the complex plane.Based on the order of the object model and on the conditions for solving the system of algebraic equations, the physical realizability of the control algorithm, and the robustness of the system, the desired polynomial of the synthesized system of the corresponding degree is constructed. This polynomial contains two polynomials with unknown coefficients, and the degrees of the unknown polynomials as well as the degree of the desired polynomial are determined.From the specified damping ratio and settling time, the dominant poles of the synthesized system are determined, and on their basis the characteristic polynomial of the closed-loop system is constructed. If necessary, additional real poles located on the negative real semi-axis are added as far as possible from the dominant poles in order to ensure the physical realizability of the controller and to satisfy the required system performance.By equating the desired characteristic polynomial with the characteristic polynomial of the constructed system, and by matching the coefficients of the same powers of the variable s on both sides of the equality, a system of algebraic equations is obtained, from which the unknown coefficients and polynomials are determined.Based on the stable components of the object and the determined unknown polynomials, the transfer function of the controller is constructed. Examples of controller synthesis for first- to fourth-order object with astatic behavior using the proposed method are analyzed. The synthesized systems demonstrate high performance and good robustness.

title: An Intra-Area VSC-HVDC Back-to-Back Retrofit Scheme Based on Transfer Impedance
authors: Zuohong Li, Feng Li, Hua Pan, Haochun Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of power grid scale and the large-scale integration of renewable energy, short-circuit current levels in the China Southern Power Grid have increased markedly, posing severe challenges to the safe operation of densely meshed AC networks. Existing mitigation measures, including inter-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back links, are primarily designed for large-scale grid decoupling and exhibit limited effectiveness in suppressing excessive short-circuit currents within high-density intra-area partitions. To address this limitation, an intra-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back retrofit scheme is proposed in this paper. Transfer impedance is introduced as a key indicator to identify candidate AC transmission lines whose replacement can effectively reduce fault current contributions to critical nodes. On this basis, a comprehensive evaluation framework is established by considering short-circuit current variation, line loading characteristics, power flow magnitude, and voltage deviation, enabling systematic comparison among multiple retrofit schemes. A typical zonal power supply model is established using the BPA platform to represent the current grid topology of the China Southern Power Grid. Three-phase short-circuit simulations are carried out to evaluate system performance before and after the proposed retrofits. Simulation results show that the intra-area VSC-HVDC back-to-back integration significantly increases transfer impedance between key nodes and surrounding network areas, resulting in a pronounced reduction of short-circuit current levels. All critical nodes are maintained within acceptable safety margins while normal power exchange capability and operational flexibility are preserved. The results demonstrate that the proposed planning approach and evaluation framework provide an effective and practical solution for mitigating short-circuit currents in large-scale AC grids with dense internal coupling.

title: Reconstruction of Scan Scintigraphy and SPECT Images Using an Enhanced Iterative Blind Deconvolution Algorithm
authors: Letícia Alves Ferreira, Sérgio Querino Brunette, Bárbara Juarez Amorim, Eduardo Tavares Costa
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Image restoration is the process of estimating the original image content from a degraded image. It is assumed the main type of scintigraphy (scan) and SPECT images degradation is due to the Compton effect. In this paper we present a methodology for mitigating this effect by image deconvolution using the system PSF and dependent on the type of radiotracer and the patient phenotype. The methodology applies a blind deconvolution using Richardson-Lucy iterative algorithm along with other techniques, like automatic iterations interruption. Our approach consists of estimating the total PSF of the patient-detector system applying the blind deconvolution twice, using the PSF found on the first deconvolution, which has as parameters the lower energy window (Compton effect) phantom image and the image of the point source filled with the radiotracer of interest (<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">99m</sup>Tc MDP, <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">177</sup>Lut and <sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">131</sup>I). The methodology was applied on scintigraphy and SPECT images of the phantom Quality Nema-NU2-2007 filled with water and one of the radiotracers. Reconstructed images of the phantom presented a reduced Compton effect and a better edge definition, after analyzing the ECBM metric (related to the borders definition) - any gain greater than zero represents a better border definition. This metric presented a gain of over 100% in most of the images.

title: An Interpretable and Efficient KNN-Based Approach for Object Segmentation
authors: Sina Saadati, Abdolah Sepahvand
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Computer vision and image segmentation play a critical role in diverse applications, including industry, urban infrastructure, and medical procedures. Deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs) are widely used for object segmentation; however, they demand high computational resources, leading to increased energy consumption. In this study, we propose a lightweight KNN-based algorithm for image segmentation that reduces computational cost. The method requires the user to select a few points around the target object, after which the algorithm automatically segments the object of interest with minimal computation. Since many objects can be distinguished by color, the proposed approach enables efficient image annotation and segmentation, producing labeled data that can be used to train deep CNNs. This method offers a practical solution for reducing computational overhead while supporting computer vision tasks.

title: Mathematical Models and System-Level Analysis of Modern Geolocation Technologies
authors: Lilia Rotaru, Irina Cojuhari, Maria Gutu, Artur Kazak
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Accurate geolocation is essential for modern cyber-physical systems supporting applications such as autonomous navigation, intelligent transportation, mobile communications, and Internet of Things infrastructures. Contemporary solutions rely on complementary technologies, including Global Navigation Satellite Systems (GNSS), terrestrial wireless networks, and inertial sensors. This paper presents a theoretical system-level analysis of major geolocation technologies and their mathematical models, examining GNSS trilateration with clock bias, multi-constellation architectures, RSSI-based cellular and Wi-Fi localization, and inertial navigation. Estimation frameworks, including weighted least-squares and Kalman filtering for multi-sensor fusion, are also analyzed. A comparative evaluation of satellite, terrestrial, and inertial approaches highlights their operational characteristics, error sources, and accuracy regimes, emphasizing the importance of hybrid architectures for reliable positioning across diverse environments.

title:
CO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    emission prediction based on the Type 2 Fuzzy Recurrent Neural Network (T2FRNN)
authors: Moammad Fiuzy, Saber Ghasemiyan Mazaher, Ali Mousavi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The deterioration of atmospheric quality has become a pressing global environmental concern. The focus has always been on reducing or avoiding environmentally harmful gas emissions. This study introduces an original methodology for estimating levels of carbon dioxide emissions. A new modeling framework based on a Type-2 Fuzzy Recurrent Neural Network (T2FRNN) is proposed, using real-world data obtained from Delhi, one of the world’s most heavily polluted urban areas. We have also presented the new learning algorithm for this purpose and also proposed a method that has shown satisfactory prediction accuracy, with a noteworthy value of 10units for its prediction RMSE error, signifying its effective functioning.

title: A Multi-Criterion Taxonomy of Cybersecurity Beneficiaries
authors: Svetlana Cojocaru, Alexandru-Andrei Ghita, Ludmila Peca, Marius Dumitrascu, Rostislav Călin
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Cyber-attacks generate increasingly large global losses while financial resources for securing information systems remain limited. Existing reference frameworks (CIS Controls, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST CSF) lack fine-grained differentiation between beneficiary types, leading to inefficient resource allocation. This paper proposes a multi-criterion taxonomy consisting of ten organizational categories (O1–O10) and five population categories (P1–P5). The taxonomy extends the preliminary categorization matrix from [1], which was based solely on organizational size and occupational status, by introducing three additional classification criteria: sectoral risk profiles derived from attack frequency data, alignment with national legislative requirements (Republic of Moldova), and digital literacy assessment for population categories. Organizational classification employs EU SME definitions, while a four-level cybersecurity scale (Ordinary, Medium, Enhanced, High) correlates with CIS Controls implementation groups for practical application. Comparative analysis demonstrates advantages in sectoral granularity, population coverage, and applicability under resource constraints. The paper acknowledges the theoretical nature of the framework and presents a detailed three-stage validation protocol for future empirical testing, with limitations explicitly identified and primary contributions being conceptual and methodological.

title: System Hang reduction on TEL Track Act8 Tools: A Case Study on Legacy Slave HDD Failure
authors: Shine Arkar Aung, Liu Houdong, Fazuli Sani
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Intermittent system hangs on TEL Track Act8 tools were causing unpredictable production interruptions despite repeated software reimaging, firmware alignment, and controller replacement. A structured root-cause investigation revealed that aging secondary ("slave") hard disk drives—originally intended only for local logging—were injecting intermittent timing disturbances and power-rail ripple during retry events. These effects degraded controller timing margins, triggering watchdog resets and system hangs. We combined field data, Self-Monitoring, Analysis, and Reporting Technology (SMART) attribute trend analysis, oscilloscope-based power integrity measurements, and reliability modeling using Mean Time Between Failure (MTBF) and Arrhenius acceleration to characterize the degradation mechanism. Removing the slave HDDs and migrating logging to network storage eliminated >80% of unexplained hangs across the fleet, restoring deterministic controller behavior and improving overall equipment availability. The findings highlight the reliability risks of legacy electromechanical storage within mature semiconductor tools and provide practical obsolescence-management strategies for High-Volume Manufacturing (HVM) environments.

title: AI-Enhanced Learning Environments to Foster Communicative Competence in French. A Mixed-Methods Study with STEAM Students
authors: Miriam Iliana Navarrete Bear, Adriana Morales Rodriguez, Cristian Tatiana Segura Dominguez
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: This paper presents an educational innovation project designed to enhance communicative competence in French as a Foreign Language (FLE) through the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools. Conducted between November 2024 and May 2025 at PrepaTec Campus Tampico, Tecnológico de Monterrey (Mexico), the study involved 68 students aged 1618, enrolled in the Multicultural High School Program. These learners, coming from Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics (STEAM) disciplines, participated in FLE courses at levels 2, 4, and 5, corresponding to CEFR A1-B1. The project integrated applications such as ChatGPT, TecGPT, Hello History, Fliki, HeyGen, Suno, Piclumen, Hailou, and SkillStudio to strengthen oral and written production in digitally enriched learning environments. A mixed-methods approach was applied: quantitative data were obtained through pre- and post-tests (TCF and DELF-style simulations), while qualitative insights emerged from student feedback, audio/video productions, and teacher observations. Results indicate significant gains in fluency, pronunciation, argumentation, accuracy, and learner autonomy, with increased motivation and confidence. This study demonstrates that AI-enhanced environments can foster communicative competence in non-immersive contexts, offering replicable models for integrating emerging technologies into language education.

title: VPETD: A Generative AI Driven Novel Privacy Themed Cybersecurity Educational Game
authors: Abhishek Shah, Ankur Chattopadhyay, Levi Pfantz
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: This paper introduces a first of its kind generative artificial intelligence (AI) driven visual privacy themed educational game (VPETD), which is an enhanced version of the Visual Privacy Enhancing Technology (VPET) themed game. The VPETD game was designed to teach data privacy plus security concepts in an interactive and hands-on learning mode. Building upon the original VPET game, VPETD makes innovative use of generative AI in the form of Stable Diffusion, which is a text-to-image generator model, to create diverse character sets. VPETD introduces a novel Doppelganger feature, that enables players to select lookalike disguises using a slider-based interface. This Doppelganger functionality helps improve the game engagement and experience with a redesigned user interface and dynamically variant image sets. VPETD employs computer vision-based image processing techniques (via OpenCV) and multimedia synthesis to support de-identification driven image obfuscation tasks and to illustrate privacy through visual anonymity as an essence of how VPET works. To assess the potential value and efficacy of VPETD, we performed a user assessment study with a diverse set of participants, including K-12 learners plus college-level students, as well as educators, who played the game in our cybersecurity training camps and workshops. This paper shares and analyzes the preliminary data collected from these participants in the form of survey responses, as part of this conducted research study. Our study demonstrates the prospects of VPETD, increased participant interest plus awareness levels in privacy and security, plus a high recommendation rate for our game. In summary, our findings show that VPETD raises overall awareness plus understanding levels and drives meaningful engagement, thus highlighting its utility as a generative AI-powered educational game in an experiential learning setup.

title: Interactive Graphical User Interface for Spectral Doppler Simulation and Experimentation
authors: Gilson M. Kanashiro, Joaquim M. Maia, Amauri A. Assef, Eduardo T. Costa
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: This paper presents an interactive computational environment developed for spectral Doppler simulation and experimentation, integrating B-mode image reconstruction and blood flow analysis. The system was implemented in MATLAB and provides customization features, including dynamic adjustment of insonation angle, spectral resolution, dynamic range, and baseline shift. Its modular architecture enables exploration of each stage of the Doppler pipeline - from raw data acquisition to spectral visualization - making it an effective tool for education and research in biomedical engineering. Preliminary results demonstrate that the environment enhances understanding of fundamental concepts of ultrasound signal processing and important principles such as aliasing, the relationship between pulse repetition frequency and resolution, and the impact of angle steering of plane waves on velocity estimation.

title: A Distribution Network Situation Awareness Method Based on Multi-Time-Scale Hybrid Measurements
authors: Yan Liu, Jiaxiao Wang, Yanyan Liang, Chengcheng Wu, Peng Xu, Yaya Xu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the rapid development of active distribution networks, measurement data sources have become increasingly diverse, and different types of data are updated at inconsistent rates. Therefore, characterizing uncertainty and achieving reliable situation awareness under multi-time-scale hybrid measurements has become an urgent challenge. This paper first proposes a distribution-network situation awareness method based on multi-time-scale hybrid measurements. To fuse real-time data, historical data, and short-term forecast data, a sliding-window mechanism is used to temporally align them to a unified evaluation instant. Then, affine arithmetic is adopted to model multi-source uncertainties in a unified manner: correlation among data from different sources is captured via shared error terms, while random errors are described by the inherent noise of each data source, ultimately transforming uncertainty into interval-valued state estimates. Finally, a risk situation index is constructed based on the upper and lower bounds of the voltage intervals, and comparative experiments are conducted on the improved IEEE 13-bus and improved IEEE 33-bus test systems. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed method improves state prediction accuracy under incomplete measurements and correlated errors, providing interpretable situation information and technical support for distribution-network operational risk assessment and active dispatch.

title: Research on Control Strategy for Seamless Transition Between Grid-Connected and Islanded Modes
authors: ZhiWei Wang, Xu Jun Ma, Xiao Wei Li, Kai Zhang, XuHuan Zhao, Cheng Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Seamless transitions between grid-connected and islanded modes in hybrid microgrids are critical for uninterruptible power and system stability. However, dynamic mode transitions frequently cause voltage flicker, transient current surges, and phase jumps. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a comprehensive seamless transition control strategy for T-type neutral-point-clamped (NPC) energy storage converters. For the grid-to-island transition, an improved dual-loop control method with load current feedforward is introduced. Pre-setting the integrator states ensures continuous controller output across the transition boundary, effectively suppressing DC-bus voltage fluctuations and transient current surges. Conversely, during the island-to-grid recovery, a real-time phase pre-synchronization loop adjusts the converter’s output angular frequency to track the grid phase. This guarantees smooth and continuous phase alignment before the dynamic reconnection. Underpinning these strategies, the control loops are modeled and parameterized using standard Type-I and Type-II system theories. Simulation results verify that the proposed approach successfully eliminates transient surges and achieves high-quality bidirectional seamless transitions.

title: Innovative, effective and cost saving strategies for post implant ashing wet clean and post poly etch wet clean
authors: Janice Koh, Zhenjie Zhu, Sicong Chen, Tek Sing Lim, Christopher Lim
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents the organic residue removal performance using low-concentration O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> water in place of Sulfuric Peroxide Mixture (SPM) in post implant and poly etch wet clean. The replacement demonstrated better performance in Yield and Defect Density (YDD), Electrical Test (ET) and sort. With this replacement, we can save approximately 55 % of our chemical cost.

title: An Ensemble Deep Learning Framework for Precise Diagnosis of Lung Diseases Using Medical Imaging
authors: Nasibeh Termeh Mousavi, Azamossadat Nourbakhsh, Asadollah Shahbahrami
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Due to the rising burden of respiratory pathologies and the critical necessity for swift, precise diagnostics, especially amidst crises like the COVID-19 pandemic, this research introduces a hybrid deep learning architecture rooted in ensemble techniques for detecting lung disorders via CT and chest X-ray imagery. The methodology incorporates a bilateral filter to suppress noise without compromising edge integrity. Subsequently, Mask R-CNN is deployed to segment potential regions of interest (ROI), followed by feature extraction utilizing the VGG16 backbone. Classification is handled by an optimized ensemble of CNN classifiers with fine-tuned hyperparameters. Empirical outcomes reveal a marked enhancement, attaining 98% accuracy and sensitivity, thereby surpassing conventional baseline models in diagnosing COVID-19, pneumonia, and lung cancer. By merging sophisticated image processing with deep ensemble learning, this framework exhibits significant promise for clinical deployment.

title: First-Year Engineering Design: Active Learning with Rockets, Simulation, and AI
authors: Jorge Alvarez, Jorge Hidalgo, Juan-Gabino Diaz-Martinez, Irandi Gutiérrez-Carmona, Zahira Gabriela Cruz-Netro, L.F. Guevara-Chavez
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: This paper presents the design, implementation, and evaluation of the HydroRocket project, a project-based learning (PBL) experience for first-year engineering students. The activity combined the construction of a water-propelled rocket with digital simulation in MATLAB, data acquisition through Arduino-based sensors, and personalized tutoring supported by artificial intelligence. The primary objective was to enhance engineering design self-efficacy, defined as students' confidence in their ability to address complex design tasks. The validated Engineering Design Self-Efficacy Scale was administered to 62 students, yielding mean scores ranging from 3.56 to 4.05 with high internal consistency <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\alpha=0.91)$</tex>. Quantitative results were reinforced by qualitative evidence. Additionally, prototype performance improved by 44% over successive iterations, providing objective evidence of design improvement. Findings indicate that the integration of emerging technologies into hands-on projects can foster motivation, autonomous learning, and the development of professional identity in engineering from the earliest stages of education.

title: The Development of Foundations of Open Practices Online Courses
authors: Diana Andone, Vlad Mihaescu, Radu Vasiu, Silviu Vert, Andrei Ternauciuc, Marius Tataru
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: This paper introduces the analyses of the design, create, and pilot four modular courses - Licensing and Open Licensing, Open Education, Open Access and Open Science, and Open Innovation, as integrated in a micro-specialisation of Foundation of Open Practices - to equip students and staff across the E<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>UDRES<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> European Universities Alliance with the skills, knowledge, and credentials needed to engage with open practices. The course development methodology involved a collaborative, cross-institutional design process based on the EU DigComp 2.2 Digital Competences framework, Design-Based Research (DBR), intersected with the ABCtoVLE method and refined based on instructional design principles and the Open Educational Practices (OEP) framework. The integration of the Foundations in Open Practices training programme into the E<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>UDRES<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> Arena Virtual Learning Environment (VLE), built on Moodle, represents a strategic step toward creating a coherent, scalable, and pedagogically aligned digital learning ecosystem for these courses. The courses contribute to embedding openness within higher education curricula and align with European digital competence frameworks.

title: Fast Partitioning Strategy for Three-Phase Unbalanced Distribution Networks Based on Electrical Coupling Strength
authors: Jiaxiao Wang, Yan Liu, Yanyan Liang, Can Liu, Yaya Xu, Yan Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasingly widespread application of distributed computing in distribution network analysis, efficient network partitioning methods have become a crucial foundation for supporting parallel computing and distributed state estimation. This paper proposes a partitioning strategy suitable for three-phase unbalanced distribution networks. Based on the inherent electrical characteristics of the network, the strategy constructs a partitioning model for distribution network scenarios without distributed generation. First, the electrical distance is defined using the node impedance matrix to quantify the coupling strength between nodes. On this basis, a community detection algorithm with modularity optimization is adopted for initial partitioning, resulting in multiple highly cohesive initial subareas. Subsequently, a merging strategy is proposed, which iteratively merges the adjacent subareas with the strongest electrical coupling and the smallest scale until a predefined number of partitions is achieved. This ensures tight electrical connections within each subarea while maintaining relatively balanced scales across subareas.

title: Joint Frequency-Phase Estimation of Power Grid Signals Based on Frequency-Domain Attention Mechanism and Kalman Filter Synergy
authors: Junhao Feng, Tongyin Dai, Chen Liu, Shuguang Liu, Xiandong Zhang, Shuai Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Grid frequency is a core indicator of power quality. Its precise measurement serves as the benchmark for safe and stable grid operation while directly impacting the control accuracy of renewable energy grid integration. The increasing non-stationary characteristics of renewable power systems lead to frequent frequency fluctuations. Frequency measurement faces challenges such as slow dynamic response, cumulative phase errors, and low accuracy under scenarios involving sudden frequency changes, phase jumps, and harmonic pollution. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a joint frequency-phase estimation method for grid signals based on the synergy of frequency-domain attention mechanisms and Kalman filtering. This approach constructs a lightweight neural network architecture that extracts joint time-frequency domain features through cross-timescale convolutional layers. A frequency-domain attention module is designed to adaptively enhance response weights for fundamental and key harmonic components, effectively suppressing high-frequency noise and irrelevant frequency interference. Physical constraint phase encoding and Kalman dynamic correction mechanisms are introduced to eliminate phase jump errors and mitigate window truncation effects. Experimental validation demonstrates superior tracking accuracy and dynamic response performance under complex disturbance conditions.

title: A Learning Framework for Control Systems in Manufacturing Processes Using the FrED Platform
authors: David Ángeles-Rojas, Pedro Ponce, Bradley Russel, Brian Anthony
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: This paper presents a training framework using the Fiber Extrusion Device (FrED) from MIT to enhance manufacturing process education and strengthen the connection between control theory and practical implementation. A common challenge in engineering education is that students often struggle to relate theoretical concepts to real-world systems. This work addresses that gap through an integrated experimental approach. The framework links academic learning with industrial practice by guiding participants through data acquisition, system identification, and PID controller design using FrED's actuator. Experimental data are modeled with the least squares method to obtain a second order transfer function, allowing learners to observe how parameter estimation and gain tuning influence system behavior. Using MATLAB and Python for analysis and implementation, the methodology improves both conceptual understanding and practical competence, contributing to workforce development in smart manufacturing.

title: Multi-Time-Scale Flexible Regulation Capability Evaluation and Bi-Level Optimal Configuration of Integrated Energy Systems Considering an Improved Sparrow Search Algorithm
authors: Yishu Kong, Guangqian Ding, Jinhan Zhao, Zhuoqun Wu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of insufficient flexibility and high penalty costs caused by severe source-load fluctuations in high-penetration renewable energy microgrids . This paper proposes a bi-level optimization configuration model that takes into account flexibility across multi-time-scales. The upper level employs improved sparrow search algorithm(ISSA) for global optimization of flexibility resource capacity, while the lower level relies on MILP to realize refined day-ahead and intra-day multi-time-scale scheduling. The study analyzed the trans-medium flexibility mechanism under multi-physics field coupling. Through simulation, it revealed the envelope accommodation capability of the low-inertia electrolyzer at noon for the overflow of photovoltaic power, as well as the economic arbitrage phenomenon where redundant electrical energy is transferred to P2G hydrogen production under the rigid constraints of the system at night. The results show that, compared with the traditional single- level planning, the ISSA-MILP bi-level framework proposed in this paper reduces the penalty for short-term flexibility deficiency and achieves high proportion of local consumption of new energy, and lowers the comprehensive annualized cost of the system over its full life cycle. This research provides quantitative theoretical support for the flexible autonomy and economic operation of future integrated energy systems(IES).

title: Research Progress on Flexible Interconnection Devices for Active Distribution Networks
authors: Ran Chen, Peng Zhang, Wei Bao, Chengzhi Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The continuous growth of distributed generation and loads is driving the evolution of distribution networks towards greater flexibility and intelligence. As a key enabler of this transformation, flexible interconnection devices can effectively enhance the controllability, reliability, and renewable energy hosting capacity of distribution networks. This paper firstly outlines the basic structures and operational principles of typical flexible interconnection devices. Subsequently, it systematically summarizes the current research status and challenges in key technologies within the planning and design dimension. Finally, the paper delves into the future application bottlenecks and development trends in this field, aiming to provide valuable references for in-depth research and the scaled deployment of flexible interconnected distribution networks.

title: A Cross-Validation Framework for Robust ANN Modeling: Application to Electrical Discharge Machining of Inconel 718
authors: Mohammad Hossein Aminian, Arash Khosravi, Ahmad Azarnik
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Artificial Neural Networks are widely used for modeling nonlinear processes in experimental fields such as machining, where datasets are often small and noisy. However, many studies rely on weak baseline models, manual single train–test splits, and unclear hyperparameter tuning, which limit reproducibility and model reliability. This work introduces a systematic and reproducible pipeline for ANN training and evaluation under data-scarce conditions. The framework integrates k-fold cross-validation, structured hyperparameter tuning, and a balanced-score selection criterion that accounts for both predictive accuracy and generalization stability. The approach is applied to Electrical Discharge Machining of Inconel 718, comparing traditional backpropagation with two metaheuristic optimizers—Genetic Algorithm and Gray Wolf Optimization. Results show that while backpropagation achieves the lowest training error, metaheuristic training offers improved robustness and stability across folds. The proposed framework minimizes bias from arbitrary data splits and provides a transparent, fair basis for evaluating ANN-based models in small experimental datasets.

title: Triboelectric Charges induced from Detaping and the Influencing Factors
authors: Ke Kian Seng Joseph
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a study on the charges induced by de-taping process because of the electrostatic discharge (ESD) events caused by the separation of insulating tape from the wafer surface. The removal process induced the triboelectrification on the tape and wafer. High drain current with outliers was observed on a NMOS depletion transistor at the Process Control Monitoring (PCM) structure as a result of these charges. Several design of experiments were carried out to investigate the factors that influence these charges and the result are discussed and illustrated in this paper.

title: Optimizing Municipal Teams: Personalized Learning for T-Shaped Profile Development
authors: Klára Kubíčkova, Janka Marschalková, Zuzana Hruška, Leonard Walletzký
publish: 2026 Smart City Symposium Prague (SCSP)
abstract: As Smart Cities mature from technological pilots to complex Cooperative Cities, the primary barrier to success is often not technical, but organizational. Traditional municipal structures rely on vertical silos that inhibit the crossdepartmental collaboration required for climate neutrality and system-level resilience. This paper argues that the Cooperative City requires a fundamental shift in human capital development toward T-shaped professionals-experts who combine deep vertical specialization with broad horizontal skills in boundary spanning and strategic collaboration. We present findings from a qualitative study of Czech municipalities identifying a critical gap in these horizontal competencies and a reactive approach to upskilling. In response, we introduce a Proof of Concept for an AI-driven system and explore the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) to transform municipal education. We specifically identify the challenges of moving beyond static recommendations toward automated, individualized content generation. By synthesizing practical best practice examples into personalized learning paths, we demonstrate a vision for a “human infrastructure” that fosters true inter-institutional cooperation.

title: Aligning Engineering and Technology Management Education with AI-Driven Industry Expectations
authors: Ehud Menipaz, Natalia Bukhshtaber, Ben Bulmash
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: The paper examines how the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) is reshaping work practices, elevating performance expectations, and redefining competencies in engineering and technology management. Drawing on interviews with industry professionals, it identifies a cognitive shift toward human-AI co-thinking as the mechanism driving these transformations. The study links empirical findings to a conceptual framing that explains why and how AI alters practices, performance benchmarks, and skill demands within hybrid socio-technical systems, and translates these insights into recommendations for curricula, pedagogy, and assessment. By exposing the widening gap between industry requirements and educational provision, it underscores the urgency of reform. The paper calls for systemic change to prepare graduates not only to function effectively in AI-augmented workplaces but also to lead innovation and socio-technical transformation in the AI era.

title: IEEE CTSoc World Newsletter
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Consumer Electronics Magazine

title: Stability Analysis of Control Systems with First-order Reset Elements, a Frequency-Domain Approach
authors: Ali Hosseini, Hassan HosseinNia
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Reset control systems (RCSs) can achieve performance beyond that of conventional linear time-invariant (LTI) controllers, while also allowing analysis directly in the frequency domain using measured frequency response functions (FRFs). Despite this potential, existing frequency-domain stability approaches are typically restricted to specific RCS architectures and commonly depend on parametric plant models, which limits their applicability in practice. In this paper, a generalized Hβ framework is developed for the most comprehensive class of RCS structures, incorporating pre-, post-, and parallel LTI filters, as well as nonzero after-reset values. Based on this formulation, an FRF-based representation corresponding to the Hβ transfer function is derived, and frequency-domain sufficient conditions are established to certify the Hβ-based quadratic stability criterion. As a result, the proposed framework enables direct FRF-based assessment of quadratic stability and convergence for the considered class of reset control systems, using the measured plant FRF together with the known controller and filter transfer functions, without requiring an explicit parametric plant model. The effectiveness and practical relevance of the method are demonstrated through an illustrative industrial case study.

title: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters Information for Authors
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Photonics Technology Letters

title: MmWave NLOS Sensing under Rough Relay Surface: Challenges and Solutions
authors: Guanghua Liu, Xiaotong Lu, You Xu, Haoran Yuan
publish: IEEE Aerospace and Electronic Systems Magazine
abstract: Non-Line-of-Sight (NLOS) perception is essential for preventing potential collisions and finds applications in areas such as autonomous driving, target localization, and human recognition in urban environments. Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar distinguishes itself by robust NLOS sensing abilities. However, in urban environments, the reflective properties of relay surfaces have become complex. Diverse construction materials, intricate architectural designs, and dynamic environmental variations introduce complexity, violating the conventional assumption of smooth, deterministic geometries and degrading NLOS perception performance. To improve mmWave NLOS sensing under these realistic conditions, we investigate the characteristics of rough relay surfaces and develop a sensing framework to manage multipath interference. Our approach specifically focuses on three specific challenges often overlooked in current research: precise detection under small-scale rough relay surfaces, high-resolution imaging under large-scale relay surfaces, and multipath scattering classification under uncertain surfaces. We establish scattering signal models under different roughness conditions and develop effective NLOS sensing systems based on the distribution characteristics of targets behind different rough surfaces, achieving accurate and reliable sensing.

title: Data-Driven Optimized Output Regulation for Markov Jump Linear Systems and Its Application
authors: Hao Shen, Zheng Huang, Jiacheng Wu, Jing Wang, Jinde Cao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cybernetics
abstract: The linear optimized output regulation problem (LOORP) for Markov jump linear systems (MJLSs) is investigated in this article. First, by improving previous methods for solving the regulator equations, a model-based scheme is proposed to address the LOORP of MJLSs. Subsequently, a reinforcement learning (RL)-based iteration scheme is developed, which enables the solution of the LOORP even when the system dynamics are partially unknown and the initial control gains are unstable. Finally, an LCL-coupled inverter-based distributed generation system is provided to demonstrate the performance of the proposed RL-based scheme.

title: Message from the Publication Chair 2026 IEEE World Engineering Education Conference (EDUNINE)
authors: N/A
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: On behalf of the publication committee, I am delighted to welcome you to our Edunine 2026, the tenth edition in Mexico. A long history of knowledge generation in the Latin American context; also, anecdotes, memories, and, principally, we are part of a strong Educational Community. Edunine was established to contribute to our understanding of the impact of education on society and to develop methodologies and strategies in engineering education.

title: Enable Scalable and Secure ISAC-WPT in Wireless Scenarios
authors: Xiufeng Liu, Xiulong Liu, Xin Xie, Ke Wang, Jiuwu Zhang, Xinyu Tong, Zheng Gong, Keqiu Li
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) and Wireless Power Transfer (WPT) technologies enable the transmission of wireless waves to power battery-free user devices while simultaneously supporting their communication and environmental sensing functions. However, as the number of users increases in such scenarios, existing ISAC-WPT schemes face challenges of overload interference and security vulnerabilities. To this end, this paper proposes a Scalable and Secure ISAC-WPT (S²SCPT) scheme. Specifically, the uplink base station integrates OFDMA and NOMA technologies, utilizing a three-dimensional resource optimization strategy to suppress multi-user interference, which balances communication and sensing performance while supplying power to energy harvesting receivers (EHR). For security, a dynamic subcarrier hopping and directional power modulation scheme based on OFDMA is developed to enhance Physical-layer Key Generation (PKG); downlink users utilize cyclic codes and an ACK verification mechanism to locate subcarriers and protect WPT security. Experimental results indicate that, in multi-user scenarios, the proposed method increases the communication rate by up to 26% while maintaining sensing performance; during WPT, it reduces power consumption by 11% without compromising communication rates, and enhanced PKG demonstrates significant advantages in the system’s sum secret key rate.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters

title: An Improved Graphical Method for Layout Optimization of Field-Limiting Ring Edge Termination
authors: Kiyoshi Takeuchi, Munetoshi Fukui, Takuya Saraya, Kazuo Itou, Toshihiko Takakura, Shinichi Suzuki, Hiroyuki Takase, Toshiro Hiramoto
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: An improved graphical method for optimizing the layout of field-limiting rings (FLRs) for edge termination is proposed. The required number of rings and the optimal combination of spacings between adjacent rings can be efficiently determined through a simple procedure based on TCAD simulations of three-terminal model structures. In a 1.2kV-class Si diode design example, the proposed method achieved 97% of the target breakdown voltage and 92% of the ideal parallel-plate breakdown voltage using 24 rings. Acomparison with a previously proposed method and numerical optimization results is also provided to further validate the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

title: Joint Secure Localization and Attack Detection for Vehicular Networks With Quantized Measurements: A Variational Bayesian Approach
authors: Yunfei Li, Zhengyao Zhang, Yiting Luo, Weiqiang Tan, Zheng Shi, Shaodan Ma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: This letter addresses the critical challenge of secure and reliable localization in vehicular networks, where stringent bandwidth constraints and malicious attacks coexist. We propose the Quantized Variational Bayesian Learning-based Attack Detection and Secure Localization (QVBL-ADSL) algorithm, a novel method that jointly estimates the position of a target vehicle and identifies potential attacks using quantized measurements. By formulating the problem within a principled variational Bayesian framework, our approach efficiently approximates the complex posterior distribution, simultaneously handling the nonlinearities introduced by quantization and the distortions caused by adversarial interventions, while also accounting for node position uncertainty. The algorithm is computationally tractable and guaranteed to converge, making it suitable for real-time vehicular applications. Numerical simulations demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in both localization accuracy and attack detection rate compared to state-of-the-art techniques, demonstrating particular efficacy in resource-limited and adversarial settings.

title: 2026 Smart City Symposium Prague (SCSP)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: RLAPR: Reinforcement Learning-based Adaptive Fusion for Visual-LiDAR Place Recognition in Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Shuhuan Wen, Kaikang Bai, Peng Li, Qichao Zhao, Huaping Liu, F. Richard Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: Place recognition is essential for robust long-term autonomy in autonomous vehicles. Single sensor is limited for complex environments, while multi-modal fusion of camera and LiDAR is effective. Static or attention-based fusion methods struggle to adapt to dynamic environments. To address the limitation, we propose a novel reinforcement learning (RL) framework for dynamic weight adaptation in visual-LiDAR place recognition tailored for autonomous vehicle perception systems. In our approach, the RL agent acts as a meta-controller. It observes the current environmental state, which is derived from the characteristics of the input data, and learns a policy to dynamically adjust the contribution weights of the visual and LiDAR feature streams. This allows the network to dynamically favor the more reliable modality, such as LiDAR in low-light conditions or camera data in geometrically sparse scenes. We train and evaluate our model extensively on the challenging nuScenes dataset, which encompasses a diverse range of driving conditions across various times of day and weather scenarios. Experimental results demonstrate that our RL-based adaptive fusion strategy consistently outperforms existing static and attentional fusion methods, achieving superior recall rates and enhanced robustness to environmental variations for reliable vehicle navigation.

title: Multi-Static Integrated Sensing, Identification and Backscatter Communication for IoT Networks
authors: Songmin Li, Ying-Chang Liang
publish: IEEE Internet of Things Journal
abstract: In this paper, a multi-static integrated sensing, identification and backscatter communication (ISIBC) system is proposed for Internet-of-Things (IoT) networks, where the backscatter devices (BDs) attached to the targets utilize ambient radio frequency (RF) signals for data transmission and multiple receivers are used for target localization. Each receiver estimates the directions of arrival (DoAs) of the incident signals from the RF source, the targets with BDs and the environmental scatterers, detects the activities of each BD, and demodulates the symbols transmitted by each active BD. With the DoA estimation and activity detection results, the receivers are required to identify the targets, i.e., associating the estimated DoAs with the corresponding targets attached with active BDs. These tasks, however, are challenging as the ambient RF signal is unknown. To tackle this issue, we propose a semi-blind generalized likelihood ratio test (GLRT) detector to estimate the activities of the BDs, which tests the homogeneity of the received signal samples over different BD symbol periods without requiring any information about the ambient RF signal and backscattering channels. A constant false alarm rate (CFAR) threshold is derived for the proposed GLRT detector. Further, we propose a novel semi-blind eigenvector-based identification method to identify the targets without using knowledge of the ambient RF signal. The eigenvector-based demodulator is proposed to demodulate the symbols transmitted by active BDs. Finally, the estimation and identification results from each receiver are utilized to jointly localize the active BDs. Extensive simulation results are presented to show the effectiveness of the proposed methods for the studied ISIBC system.

title: Meta-Learning-Enhanced Task Assignment and Resource Scheduling for UAV-Assisted WSNs in 6G-Enabled ITS
authors: Mesfin Leranso Betalo, Amr Mohamed, Amin Sharafian, Zongze Wu, Jianqiang Li, Xiaoshan Bai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Mobile Computing
abstract: The integration of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), wireless sensor networks (WSNs), and 6 G technologies is transforming the design of next-generation Intelligent Transportation Systems (ITS), enabling real-time, energy-efficient, and adaptive urban mobility services. This paper proposes a novel meta-learning-enhanced UAV-assisted WSN architecture, tailored for large-scale, dynamic ITS environments characterized by high vehicular mobility, dense sensor deployments, and stringent latency requirements. We introduce a new task assignment framework to address critical challenges such as coverage limitations at urban intersections, energy constraints of sensor nodes, and the need for rapid decision-making under complex traffic conditions. We aim to maximize energy-efficient data throughput (EEDT) and ensuring Quality of Service (QoS) in UAV-assisted WSNs. The framework jointly optimizes traffic sensor node selection, UAV trajectory planning, and communication resource allocation by formulating the problem as a Constrained Markov Decision Process (CMDP). To solve this, we develop the Meta-Learning Weighted Multi-Agent Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (MW-MAD3PG) algorithm, which embeds model-agnostic meta-learning (MAML) within a cooperative multi-agent reinforcement learning (MADRL) setting. MW-MAD3PG enables UAV agents to rapidly adapt to dynamic traffic patterns, fluctuating road conditions, and evolving network states with minimal retraining, ensuring high energy efficiency and system scalability. Extensive simulations show that the proposed framework achieves up to a 25% improvement in UAV coordination efficiency, a 30% increase in data offloading capacity, and substantial enhancements in energy-aware operations compared to baseline methods such as MADDPG, Meta-SGD, and Meta-Q-Learning. These results validate our architecture and framework as robust, intelligent solutions for future 6G-enabled ITS, supporting resilient, real-time, and energy-optimized traffic monitoring and control.

title: CEEPE 2026 Cover Page

title: Alongamento passivo de alta intensidade no músculo antagonista gera alterações no comportamento das unidades motoras do músculo agonista durante contração isométrica? Um estudo preliminar
authors: André Brand Bezerra Coutinho, Hélio de Veiga Cabral, Liliam Fernandes de Oliveira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Static stretching is commonly used to increase range of motion (ROM) and prepare the neuromuscular system for exercise. While prolonged stretching of agonist muscles may impair performance, the effects of stretching antagonist muscles on agonist behavior remain unclear. This preliminary study investigated whether high-intensity passive stretching of the triceps surae (antagonist) alters the motor unit behavior of the tibialis anterior (agonist) during isometric dorsiflexion. Five healthy male participants performed maximal voluntary contractions and submaximal trapezoidal torque tasks before and after a stretching protocol consisting of five 45-s passive stretches separated by 15-s rest. High-density surface electromyography (HDsEMG) was recorded from the tibialis anterior and decomposed to identify motor unit discharge times, allowing matched motor units to be tracked across conditions. Peak torque and ROM were compared pre- and post-stretching, and motor unit parameters were analyzed using linear mixed-effects models. Stretching significantly increased ROM (p = 0.048) but did not significantly change peak torque (p = 0.107). No significant differences were observed in mean discharge rate (p = 0.28) or discharge rate variability (p = 0.08), although variability showed a tendency to increase. These findings suggest that high-intensity antagonist stretching may induce subtle neural adjustments in the agonist muscle.
