title: Low-Voltage Ride-Through Method for Virtual Synchronous Generators in Compliance with the New Grid Code Under Asymmetrical Faults
authors: Rui Luo, Houlei Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing penetration of inverter-based resources (IBRs), the virtual synchronous generator (VSG), as a grid-forming IBR providing inertia and grid-strength support, has attracted significant attention. However, IBRs inherently exhibit a limited overcurrent capability, typically below 1.2 p.u. Therefore, a dedicated low-voltage ride-through (LVRT) strategy is required to prevent inverter damage due to excessive fault currents. Moreover, recently issued grid codes (GCs) mandate that IBRs generate both positive-and negative-sequence reactive currents under asymmetrical fault conditions. To address these requirements, this paper proposes a LVRT strategy that complies with the new GCs without the need for auxiliary controllers, such as virtual impedance controllers. In the proposed method, the positive-sequence reactive current is regulated by adjusting the Q–u droop coefficient, while the negative-sequence reactive current is controlled by the current controller. Simulation results of a grid-tied VSG demonstrate that the proposed method achieves effective current limiting while satisfying the GC requirements.

title: Forecasting Accuracy Assessment of Average Vehicle Speeds Derived from Floating Car Data on the D11 Motorway: From ARIMA to ARIMAX
authors: František Kekula, Pavel Hrubeš
publish: 2026 Smart City Symposium Prague (SCSP)
abstract: Monitoring traffic flow conditions is crucial for quantifying transportation performance and making transportation decisions. Vehicle speed appears to be an essential traffic parameter that can be used to evaluate the performance of different road segments. This study investigates the reliability of the vehicle speed model derived from Floating Car Data (FCD) on the sample from the D11 motorway in the Czech Republic through ARIMA and ARIMAX time series forecasting. For the forecasting accuracy assessment, the average speeds from automatic traffic counters (ATCs) were used as ground truth data. First, the augmented Dickey-Fuller test was conducted to test the time series stationarity. After the testing, the autocorrelation functions were calculated to check whether the series are highly autocorrelated. Since the calculated autocorrelation functions proved highly autocorrelated time series, the pre-whitening method was applied in order to make a more reliable subsequent cross-correlations. Finally, the ARIMA and ARIMAX models were fitted. The residual diagnostic indicates that the ARIMAX model outperforms the ARIMA model and therefore improves prediction capabilities with the use of the vehicle speed model. The prediction performance varies between the different lanes of the motorway.

title: Performance Evaluation of Java/Spring Boot Location-Tracking MVP Based on Relational Databases
authors: Volodymyr Franiv, Roman Oliinyk, Mykola Brych, Oleksii Kovalisko, Oleg Kushnir, Vasyl Vasyuta
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Location-tracking applications (LTAs) are widely used in modern digital systems and are often developed as minimum viable products (MVPs) by IT startups. This paper investigates the performance characteristics of a LTA implemented using the Java programming language and the Spring Boot framework with relational databases. The efficiencies of the LTA systems based on PostgreSQL and MySQL are evaluated and compared to each other, along with two alternative data-access approaches Hibernate (JPA) and Spring JdbcTemplate.Performance experiments conducted using Apache JMeter show that the system throughput decreases with increasing dataset size according to a scalable power-law pattern, similarly to the situation observed in the earlier studies involving ASP.NET and Python/Django technology stacks. The results indicate that the Java/Spring Boot implementation provides the performance comparable to the other modern backend frameworks and represents a suitable solution for developing scalable relational-database-driven tracking MVPs. Our findings contribute to understanding technology-selection trade-offs in the design of LTA systems.

title: Intrinsic Reliability Enhancement and Elimination of Extrinsic Defects for Critical Memory Device Applications
authors: Seiichi Takedai, Adityan Murthi, John Lee, Jeff J. Ye
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper highlights the critical importance of enhancing intrinsic reliability in memory devices while preventing the introduction of extrinsic reliability defects — an essential consideration for high-quality applications such as automotive electronics. A case study is presented in which efforts to improve the intrinsic reliability of a DRAM product inadvertently resulted in a new reliability failure, caused by process-induced particle contamination. This defect, undetectable through standard electrical testing, underscores the complexity of reliability engineering. The paper details the root cause analysis and corrective actions taken to eliminate the extrinsic defect, demonstrating how intrinsic reliability improvements can be achieved without compromising overall device integrity.

title: Investigation of BEOL Wet Cleaning on Etch Stop Layer in BEOL interconnects
authors: Wei-Shang Lo, Taiki Hinode, Pijeng Khor, M. Sankarapandian, Minnal Packiam
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: With BEOL scaling, the use of the etch stop layer has become critical. In this study we investigate the impact of wet cleaning processes on the etch stop layer, focusing on the yield of via chain structures in Cu interconnects. Three different wet clean chemistries were examined: one vendor-specific formulation and two formulations based on commodity chemicals. The commodity chemical-based cleans showed lower selectivity for Co and Cu materials. In contrast, the vendor formulation exhibited higher Cu/Co selectivity with controlled etching performance on the etch stop layer. Yield analysis indicates that the vendor formulation, when paired with an optimized cleaning sequence, improves via chain functionality. This study suggests that a formulated cleaning process is required to enable reliable BEOL interconnects.

title: Research on Application Scenarios and Construction Solutions of 5G Control in Intelligent Power Plants
authors: Wei Ou, Min Gao, Qingfu Hao, Xiaojun Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Technology remains in the pilot exploration phase for real-time control applications in intelligent power plants. By analyzing the compatibility and reliability, this study proposes screening principles and recommended application scenarios for 5G control systems in intelligent power plants. For different scenarios, the research further investigates access modes of 5G instrumentation and control equipment under various 5G network conditions, as well as system construction solutions. This study contributes to expanding the application scenarios of 5G technology in real-time control for intelligent power plants and provides reference solutions for power plants to establish new 5G control systems.

title: Algorithm for Osteoarthritis of the Temporomandibular Joint Using Magnetic Resonance Imaging
authors: Carla Duque Balaguer, Mariana Silva Campos, Leticia Vilaça Willeman Bastos Tesch, Thayanne Brasil Barbosa Calcia, Ricardo Souza Tesch, Wagner Coelho De Albuquerque Pereira
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Temporomandibular joint osteoarthritis (TMJ-OA) is a heterogeneous degenerative condition involving structural alterations of cartilage, subchondral bone, and synovial tissues, frequently associated with pain and functional limitation. Growing evidence suggests that osteoarthritis comprises distinct structural phenotypes, whose identification may contribute to improved disease stratification and clinical decision-making. The aim of this methodological study was to introduce and perform an initial interobserver reliability assessment of a simplified MRI-based phenotypic classification system for temporomandibular joint osteoarthritis, termed ROAMES-TMJ (Rapid OsteoArthritis MRI Eligibility Score for the Temporomandibular Joint), originally proposed for the knee. Eighty magnetic resonance images of the temporomandibular joint from adult patients diagnosed with TMJ-OA were analyzed, comprising 20 images for each of the four predefined phenotypes. A reference standard was established through full consensus among three experienced specialists, based on independent evaluation and a structured consensus process. Subsequently, the criteria were applied in an independent and blind manner to assess interobserver agreement. Interobserver agreement yielded a weighted kappa value of 0.56 (95% CI: 0.31– 0.70), indicating moderate agreement. However, variability was observed across phenotypes: the hypertrophic phenotype showed the highest level of agreement (70.0%), while the bone phenotype showed the highest discordance (65%). These findings indicate that ROAMES-TMJ demonstrates moderate reproducibility and potential applicability as a structural stratification tool for TMJ-OA based on magnetic resonance imaging.

title:
Characterization of High-O
                    <sub>i</sub>
                    , Vacancy Rich Silicon Starting Material for Improved Thermal Slip Performance of Reduced Pressure Epitaxial Process
authors: Anya Isaacs, Majid Mansoori, Josh Lane, Hui Sheng, Albert Escusa, Thanas Budri, Ryan Smith, Bill Wofford, Steve Adler, Brad Sucher
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: As semiconductor manufacturing continues to scale to larger wafer sizes, companies face the challenge of transferring technologies which were developed at older 200 mm factories to new facilities utilizing the 300 mm wafer platform. Silicon epitaxy, a critical step in the fabrication of bipolar junction transistors and high voltage MOSFETs, enables the growth of defect free silicon layers but comes with its own sensitivities, primarily to the starting silicon substrate. One of the greatest manufacturing challenges of the epitaxy process is posed by macro plastic deformation in the form of thermal slip dislocations which can be magnified by temperature gradients across the larger surface area of a 300 mm wafer. While tight timelines are demanded for cross-fab technology transfers with a copy-exact approach, manufacturing sensitivities will require additional hardware and materials optimization to meet the stringent device performance targets. This study will explore the various equipment and starting material changes utilized to improve the thermally induced slip dislocation performance of a 300 mm polished wafer processed at high temperature and reduced pressure conditions inside a state-of-the-art 300 mm epitaxial chamber.

title: Modeling and Strategy Analysis for Timing-Sensitive Distribution Network Attacks and Defenses
authors: Yang Ding, Shengjun Huang, Qingxia Li, Xueyang Zhang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing digitalization of power systems and the growing complexity of geopolitical competition, organized and deliberate physical attack threats against distribution networks have become increasingly prominent. Traditional static attack analysis methods based on the N-k criterion struggle to characterize attackers' strategic behaviors across the temporal dimension and fail to reflect the synergistic enhancement effects between multiple attacks. This paper proposes a systematic temporal attack modeling framework. By establishing a two-layer attack-defense optimization model, it reveals the performance evolution of distribution networks under sequential malicious attacks. First, a dynamic characterization model for sequential attacks is constructed, accounting for cumulative damage effects and capacity degradation. Second, a two-layer attack-defense optimization model is formulated. Third, exhaustive search and genetic algorithm solution strategies are designed for static and sequential attacks, respectively. Finally, the proposed method is validated on an enhanced IEEE 33-node system. Experimental results demonstrate that under identical attack resources, sequential attacks inflict greater cumulative load losses than static attacks, revealing the sequential vulnerability characteristics of distribution networks under deliberate attacks. This study provides new analytical perspectives and methodological support for power system security protection.

title: Determinants of Family Members’ Adoption of Long-Term Care Mobile Applications: An Integration of TAM and ISSM
authors: Pei-Yi Lin, Wei-Min Huang, Li-Chiu Lee, Yi-Ting Lin, Chia-Fang Yang, Bing-Cong Chen
publish: 2026 11th International Conference on Information and Network Technologies (ICINT)
abstract: As Taiwan enters a super-aged society, mobile applications play a vital role in long-term care communication. This study integrates the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and Information System Success Model (ISSM) to examine family members’ adoption intentions. An online survey of 164 respondents analyzed with SEM shows that service quality and information quality enhance perceived usefulness, while system and service quality improve ease of use. Ease of use and convenience significantly drive adoption, whereas perceived usefulness and privacy risk are not significant. Results highlight convenience and service quality as key factors, guiding app design to improve usability and family–institution interaction.

title:
Comparison of ozone- and water-based Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O

                    thin films grown by atomic layer deposition
authors: Meena Rajachidambaram, Michael Aquilino, Aaron Miller, Justin Clements, Brett Yatzor, Kevin Shah, Bas Korevaar

abstract: Aluminum oxide (Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) thin films were deposited using atomic layer deposition (ALD) at 300°C with Trimethyl Aluminum (TMA) as precursor for Al, and water (H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O) and Ozone(O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf>) as oxidant. Growth rate studies show lower growth per cycle for ozone based Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> compared to water based Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> films. Precursor pulse time variation and reactor pressure variation shows a larger thickness effect for ozone based Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> films compared to water based Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> films. We also analyzed films that were pre-baked for 15min at the same conditions within the reactor as historically pre-baking helped to remove moisture and produced high quality films. Dielectric constant using mercury probe (Hg-probe) technique and Al%:O% using X-ray photo electron spectroscopy (XPS) technique did not show a difference between the two types of oxidant sources used for making Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> films. Surface roughness which was characterized using atomic force microscope (AFM) technique showed that the water-based films were less rough compared to ozone-based films, which could indicate low mobility of the limited surface hydroxyl at low temperatures for ozone-based films. Secondary Ion Mass Spectrometry (SIMS) shows less carbon at the interface of ozone-based Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> films. With increase in reactor temperature, the carbon concentration at the interface decreases. Ozone-based Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> films were further analyzed using thermal desorption spectroscopy (TDS) which showed a decrease in H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O and CO desorption peaks with increasing temperature making ozone based Al<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</inf> an effective film to be used as passivation layers and gas barriers.

title: A Robotics Middleware for FPGAs Supporting Dynamic Function Exchange and Streaming Data Distribution
authors: Alexander Philipp Nowosad, Christian Lienen, Marco Platzner
publish: 2026 IEEE 34th Annual International Symposium on Field-Programmable Custom Computing Machines (FCCM)
abstract: Field Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) have the potential to significantly improve the performance and energy efficiency of robotics applications. Several studies propose frameworks that systematically integrate FPGAs into robotic systems, primarily within the Robot Operating System (ROS), the de facto standard middleware for robotics. Recently, two main research directions have emerged: One explores the use of Dynamic Function Exchange (DFX) to time-multiplex hardware-accelerated functions on an FPGA, while the other focuses on intra-FPGA communication, leveraging interconnections between statically mapped hardware-accelerated functions to reduce communication overhead.In this paper, we present a robotics middleware that supports both DFX and dynamic intra-FPGA communication. The middleware employs a static FPGA layout with multiple reconfigurable slots, complemented by router and buffer infrastructure for streaming-based data distribution. Unlike related work, the call-backs executed in reconfigurable slots, the exchanged messages, and the routing configurations do not need to be known at build time, enabling a high degree of runtime flexibility. We describe the hardware architecture, analyze potential deadlock scenarios, and derive constraints required for deadlock-free operation, which our architecture satisfies. We evaluate the middleware using small-scale test cases and a larger autonomous robot simulation. The results quantify the overhead introduced by the flexible design compared to static mappings and demonstrate performance gains over DFX-only and software-only approaches enabled by dynamic intra-FPGA communication.

title: Big Data Analysis on Cloud and Edge Systems in the Automotive Industry: A Brief Review
authors: Marina Adriana Mercioni, Cătălin Daniel Căleanu, Mihail Gaianu, Dan Pescaru
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: The automotive industry is undergoing a significant transformation driven by Software-Defined Vehicles (SDVs) and Cooperative Intelligent Transport Systems (C-ITS). This shift has led to an exponential increase in sensory and operational data, necessitating advanced big data analytics. This review synthesizes literature from 2019 to 2026 to examine the deployment of Big Data analysis methods across Cloud and Edge computing systems. While cloud computing offers indispensable scalability for batch processing and historical data storage, it falls short in meeting ultra-low latency requirements essential for real-time autonomous driving. Consequently, Multi-access Edge Computing (MEC) and Edge AI have emerged as essential solutions for localized data processing and strategic computation offloading. Moreover, the integration of distributed vehicle architectures relies heavily on modern middleware protocols (e.g., Data Distribution Service, SOME/IP), ensuring deterministic and secure communication. This review highlights the synergy between Edge-Cloud continuums and automotive middleware by analyzing current offloading schemes, architectural topologies, and data orchestration strategies. Finally, this paper identifies critical research gaps in cognitive data sampling, dynamic resource orchestration under mixed-criticality conditions, and the lack of standardized open datasets, thereby providing a roadmap for future investigations in vehicular computing.

title: Research on Optimizing Logical Control with Optional Start-up Sequences for Multiple Pumps
authors: Xuecui Ma, Yulei Liu, Min Gao, Qingfu Hao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: In the waste liquid discharge system of the steam turbine building in a nuclear power plant, when multiple pumps are set in the same sump, the traditional liquid level control strategy can lead to frequent startups and shutdowns of a single pump. This paper designs a control method with selectable startup sequences for multiple waste liquid discharge pumps. In a sump equipped with multiple waste liquid discharge pumps, when different pumps started by liquid level at different heights, the operator can manually select the startup sequence of the first pump, and the subsequent pumps follow a fixed sequence. This method can effectively address the issue of one pump being frequently started while others are rarely started, leading to uneven pump usage rates, which is beneficial for extending equipment life. There are three aspects in this paper. First, zero-cost renovation, no additional sensors or control modules are required, and the upgrade can be achieved solely through software logic optimization. Second, balanced equipment life, by rotating the startup sequence, the operating time differences among the pumps can keep within a certain range. Third, operational flexibility, operators can dynamically adjust the strategy based on actual working conditions (such as differences in pump performance, frequency of liquid level fluctuations). In summary, through the control method designed in this paper, balanced equipment usage can be achieved without any additional investment, which extends equipment life and has practical economic benefits and market prospects.

title: Inkjet printed sol-gel titania precursor atop MEMS resonator for precise frequency control and tuning
authors: Jaime Alberto Benavides-Guerrero, Luis Felipe Gerlein, Mathieu Gratuze, Fabrice Vaussenat, Michaël Ménard, Frederic Nabki, Martin Bolduc, Sylvain G. Cloutier
publish: IEEE Sensors Journal
abstract: This article presents the design, fabrication, and characterization of microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) resonators functionalized with titanium dioxide (TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>) droplets deposited via inkjet printing technology. The integration of oxygen-deficient solgel TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> precursors onto piezoelectric MEMS resonators demonstrates a novel approach for post-processing frequency tuning and sensor functionalization. The fabricated devices consist of aluminum nitride (AlN) piezoelectric resonators manufactured using the PiezoMUMPs process, with subsequent deposition of TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> droplets using a custom-formulated ink. Experimental results demonstrate that the resonant frequency can be digitally tuned by controlling the number of deposited droplets (0-50), achieving a tuning sensitivity of about 200 Hz per droplet. The mode shape of the tuned resonator was not affected by such deposition. The performance of the modified resonator also remains stable after over 41 h of operation. The crystalline phase of the TiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> (amorphous, anatase, or rutile) was found to have a significant influence on the performance of the device . This work establishes inkjet printing as a viable, scalable, precise and partially reversible method for MEMS post-processing, opening pathways for integrated chemical sensor arrays, tunable filters, and multi-function microsystems enabling multifunctional microsystems where post-fabrication frequency tuning combines with TiO2’s sensing and photocatalytic capabilities for integrated chemical sensor arrays and self-cleaning resonant devices.

title: Identity-Based Encryption with Equality Test and Flexible Time-Based Authorization for Cloud Computing
authors: Cong Li, Xinyu Feng, Qingni Shen, Zhonghai Wu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing
abstract: Identity-based encryption with equality test (IBEET) extends identity-based encryption to support the comparison between messages in two distinct ciphertexts associated with different identities. Nevertheless, nearly all of the previous IBEET schemes have the problem that once the trapdoor is sent to the cloud server, it becomes uncontrollable. Meanwhile, the only existing IBEET scheme coping with this problem, namely IBEET with datestamp-based authorization (IBEET-DBA), suffers from the following flaws. (1) A trapdoor merely expresses a single time interval, which is inexpressive. (2) It is inflexible due to only supporting limited types of authorization. (3) It is merely proven secure in the random oracle model, which has been pointed out that it may lead to security risks in practicality. Considering the aforementioned problems, we propose an IBEET with flexible time-based authorization (IBEET-FTBA) scheme, supporting wildcards and putting no constraint on authorization types. We prove our scheme secure against adaptive chosen-ciphertext attacks in the standard model. Subsequently, we give a detailed theoretical analysis of our IBEET-FTBA construction and the main existing IBEET ones. Furthermore, we implement ours and the IBEET-DBA construction as a benchmark, and conduct numerous experiments. Both results demonstrate that ours has better performance in the scenario of multiple time intervals.

title: Efficient Hardware Architectures for AES-128, AES-192, and AES-256 Encryption_supp1-3701703.pdf
authors: Doaa Ashmawy
publish: N/A
abstract:
This paper presents efficient hardware architectures for the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) cipher supporting AES-128, AES-192, and AES-256 with On-The-Fly (OTF) key expansion units. The new designs feature low-area, low-latency OTF key expansion units that generate round keys concurrently with the data path, eliminating the need for key storage and reducing hardware overhead. By sharing logic within the key expansion circuitry, the proposed design achieves a 46% reduction in XOR gate count compared to previous works.

To optimize the architecture, we analyzed eight state-of-the-art AES S-box designs and evaluated their ASIC implementation results. Four S-boxes were selected for integration, including the most compact and the fastest designs reported in the literature. ASIC synthesis results of the AES-128 architecture shows superior performance, with the fastest design achieving 29%-79% lower Area-Delay-Power Product (ADPP) than prior designs when synthesized using the same standard cell library to ensure a fair comparison.

To support AES-192 and AES-256, we scaled the architecture by designing corresponding OTF key expansion units, while keeping the proposed AES-128 data path unchanged. The number of rounds was increased to 12 and 14, respectively. ASIC synthesis shows a proportional increase in ADPP for larger key sizes. Despite the higher hardware cost and reduced throughput for larger key sizes, the Fast architecture consistently delivers the highest performance and lowest ADPP, while the compact architecture minimizes area and power consumption. These results demonstrate flexible design trade-offs suitable for both high-performance and resource-constrained cryptographic applications.

title: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
authors: N/A
publish: N/A

title: Multiple Pinching Antennas on Multiple Waveguides for NOMA-Aided Wireless Powered Communication Network
authors: Cheng Gao, Bowen Zhao, Xiong Li, Zengbao Zhu, Qimei Cui, Xiaofeng Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Vehicular Technology
abstract: In this correspondence, we investigate a multiple pinching antennas on multiple waveguides (MPMW)-aided wireless powered communication network (WPCN). Our aim is to effectively mitigate severe energy attenuation caused by large scale fading and alleviate system unfairness resulting from the doubly near–far effect. Specifically, we formulate a max–min optimization problem to guaranty user fairness by jointly designing the active beamforming at the base station, time allocation, user transmission power, and the positions of pinching antennas (PAs). To make this non-convex problem tractable, we transform it into a maximization problem and decompose it into two subproblems. Then, we design a novel distance–phase decoupling–based alternating optimization (AO) algorithm to solve them efficiently. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly enhances user fairness relative to benchmark schemes. Moreover, these results offer valuable insights into the practical deployment strategies for waveguides and PAs, facilitating engineering implementation.

title: Physically-Based Polarization Reflection Separation for Laser Stripe Extraction on Highly Reflective Metal Surfaces_supp1-3699730.pdf
authors: Yiting Yu
publish: N/A
abstract: Polarization-based laser vision imaging offers high accuracy, non-contact sensing, and strong robustness to interference, making it promising for weld seam localization and intelligent welding. However, for planar fillet-weld workpieces with highly reflective metallic surfaces, reliable laser stripe segmentation and precise extraction of centerlines and feature points remain challenging. This is because inter-reflection interference and camera saturation severely degrade measurement accuracy. In this paper, we propose a novel physically-based polarization reflection separation method using polarization features and spatial structural information for robust laser stripe extraction. The physical principles and polarization properties of direct- and inter-reflections from metallic surfaces are investigated through theoretical modeling and simulation. By combining the Stokes parameter S2 with a saturation region segmentation criterion, the inter-reflection regions of the laser stripe are automatically determined. An adaptive mask dilation approach is then introduced to suppress saturation-induced interference without complex parameter tuning. Finally, the centerlines and feature points are accurately extracted using the gray-gravity method with least-squares fitting. Experiments demonstrate superior segmentation and extraction performance under strong reflective interference, achieving an average feature-point extraction error of 2.36 pixels, a repeatability error within ±1.16 pixels, and an average computational time of 0.2531 s. Moreover, comprehensive evaluations across varying groove angles, laser rotation angles, exposure times, surface conditions and dynamic robotic platforms further validate the robustness and effectiveness of the proposed method. This study highlights the potential of polarization imaging for addressing optical interference challenges in industrial welding scenarios, providing a solid foundation for intelligent and automated welding.

title: Joint Beamforming and Path Design for Energy-Minimized UAV-enabled ISAC System
authors: Zhiwei Pan, Haijun Zhang, Chao Ren, Yuzheng Ren, Haojin Li, Chen Sun
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Signal Processing
abstract: This paper explores the utilization of a rotary-wing UAV equipped with a vertically deployed uniform linear array (ULA) antenna as an airborne base station, enabling simultaneous downlink data transmission to ground communication users and sensing of the potential ground target. The goal is to jointly optimize the UAV trajectory and beamforming schemes to achieve minimal total energy consumption, under constraints related to communication performance, sensing accuracy, transmission power, and mobility. Due to the presence of an array antenna on the UAV, the trajectory design variables are embedded within the exponential terms of the array steering vectory, leading to strong coupling with the beamforming parameters and causing significant non-convexity in the optimization problem. To simplify the solution process, the original formulation is divided into two tractable subproblems: 1) the UAV transmit beamforming design; 2) the UAV trajectory design. A combination of mathematical tools, including semidefinite relaxation (SDR) and successive convex approximation (SCA), is employed within an alternating optimization framework to derive a computationally efficient suboptimal solution. Specifically, SDR is utilized to recast the beamforming subproblem as a semidefinite program, and SCA is further applied to convexify the resulting formulation. For the trajectory subproblem, a sequence of variable transformations is performed to decouple the trajectory variables from the exponential terms of the array steering vectory, followed by the application of SCA to obtain a convex approximation. The effectiveness of the proposed method is substantiated by simulation experiments, which demonstrate both rapid convergence and significant power saving improvements over three benchmark schemes.

title: A Novel Bridgeless Single-Stage Isolated AC–DC Converter for On-Board Charger Applications
authors: Zhuangzhuang Wei, Hanjing Dong, Xiaogao Xie, Zhenyuan Tu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Electronics
abstract: This article presents a novel bridgeless single-stage isolated AC–DC converter for on-board charger (OBC) applications. The proposed topology integrates characteristics of boost and resonant conversion within a single switching cycle, thereby achieving high voltage gain, full-range zero-voltage switching (ZVS), and a simplified circuit structure. The operational principles and design considerations are analyzed in detail. To validate the theoretical analysis, a 1.7 kW experimental prototype with an input voltage range of 185–265V<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">AC</sub> and an output voltage range of 500–850 V was developed and tested. The experimental results demonstrate that, at the rated input voltage, the converter maintains high efficiency (>96.3%) over the entire output voltage range and achieves a peak efficiency of 96.9%, making it competitive for OBC systems.

title: GS-MambaNet: A Decoupled Global-Spatial and Grouped-Spectral Mamba Network for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Dekai Li, Uzair Aslam Bhatti, Jiaxin Li, Yonis Gulzar, Shahad Albannay, Ayoob Lone
publish: IEEE Journal of Selected Topics in Applied Earth Observations and Remote Sensing
abstract: Hyperspectral images (HSIs) inherently possess abundant spectral and spatial information, making them invaluable across diverse remote sensing applications. Recently, deep learning architecture has emerged as the dominant paradigm for HSI classification. However, existing methods face an inherent dilemma: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) excel at extracting local spatial representations but fundamentally struggle to capture long-range dependencies. Conversely, Vision Transformers (ViTs) effectively capture global contexts but incur a prohibitive quadratic computational burden when processing long, continuous spectral sequences. To resolve this dual challenge, we propose GS-MambaNet, a novel decoupled architecture that synergizes Global-Spatial Attention with Grouped-Spectral State Space Modeling. Specifically, a Global-Spatial Attention Module (GSAM) is designed to capture macro-level spatial topologies, while a Grouped-Spectral State-Space Module (GSSM) adaptively partitions spectral channels into sequence groups, leveraging the linear complexity of the Mamba mechanism to efficiently model long-term spectral evolution dynamics. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets (Botswana, HongHu, HanChuan, and Pavia University) demonstrate that GS-MambaNet significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving superior classification accuracy while maintaining computational efficiency.

title: Enhanced Unified Graph Learning and Weighted Tensor Schatten $p$-Norm Approximation for Multi-View Clustering
authors: Xinyu Lan, Ming Yang, Jingyu Wang, Zhao Kang
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Multi-view clustering (MVC) is intended to enhance clustering performance by integrating complementary information from diverse sources. However, prevailing low-rank tensor based MVC methods often exhibit suboptimal performance, as they tend to treat singular values equally and lack structured sparsity constraints. A novel framework termed EUGL-WTSN is proposed to address the drawbacks of existing methods. It enhances unified graph learning and low-rank tensor learning model through the joint application of weighted tensor Schatten <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math></inline-formula>-norm and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell \_{1,2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-norm. Specifically, weighted tensor Schatten <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$p$</tex-math></inline-formula>-norm is employed in our model to more accurately capture inter-view relationships and spatial structures embedded within the multi-view data. This approach yields a tighter relaxation bound for the tensor rank through adaptive truncation of singular values. Furthermore, the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ell \_{1,2}$</tex-math></inline-formula>-norm regularization is applied to the view-specific affinity matrices to induce row sparsity, which enhances the discriminability and robustness of the learned graph structures. To optimize the proposed model, we propose an efficient iterative optimization algorithm with provable convergence guarantees. Experimental evaluations reveal that our proposed framework achieves better performance than existing methodologies, demonstrating significant improvements in clustering performance and model robustness. Codes and datasets are available: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://github.com/yangming1984/EUGL-WTSN</uri>.

title: Synchronous Stability Analysis and Enhancement of Solar–Hydro Integrated System via VSC-HVDC Transmission
authors: Liangde Xu, Zhenyong Niu, Yilin Zhong, Hongwei Zhao, Binbin Xu, Duhai Wu, Yujun Li, Zhengchun Du
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper studies the synchronization stability of a bundled power export system where a large-scale solar plant and a hydropower station are interconnected and delivered to the receiving grid through a VSC-HVDC link. Under several simplifying assumptions, the system is modeled for both normal operation and a severe three-phase fault, with the converter current-limiting behavior explicitly included because it reshapes the fault-time power transfer and directly impacts the rotor-angle dynamics of the hydropower synchronous generator. Using Thevenin’s theorem, a fault-time equivalent model is reconstructed to capture the interaction among the sending-end converter, AC network, and generator under current saturation, and the saturated-current control is parameterized by a current saturation angle that determines the active/reactive current allocation during faults. Based on the resulting simplified model, the optimal saturation angle is obtained by formulating a constrained optimization problem and solving it via the Lagrange multiplier method in combination with the equal-area criterion, aiming to maximize the transient synchronizing margin and avoid loss of synchronism. Numerical simulations validate that the derived saturation angle provides superior synchronization-stability performance compared with conventional current-limiting settings, thereby enhancing fault ride-through capability for hydro–PV bundled VSC-HVDC transmission systems.

title: Experimental and Simulation Analysis of Epoxy Resin Nanocomposites for High-Voltage Power Module Packaging
authors: Qilong Wang, Ping Zeng, Wei Bao, Desheng Zhou, Qian Feng, Jiayu Lian
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The DC electrical application potential of epoxy resin/Aluminum Nitride (AlN) nanocomposites applied for the packaging material in the double-sided cooling structure of 15 kV SiC-IGBT are evaluated in this paper based on experiment and simulation. The effect of the silicone gel and epoxy/AlN nanocomposites combined with the geometrical techniques on the electric stress reduction are compared under DC voltage with different temperatures. The influence of AlN nanoparticles on the electrical properties of epoxy resin is presented and a new physical resistive model is proposed to depict the electric field distribution in the packaging structure under DC voltage. The results show that the effect of the electrical properties of packaging materials on the electric field of the packaging structure is influenced by offset of metallization layers. When the offset of metallization layers is zero, the maximum electric stress in the silicone gel is larger than that in the epoxy/AlN nanocomposites at temperature above 60 °C. Maximum DC electric stress in epoxy/AlN nanocomposites as packaging materials is not sensitive to the temperature change. The 1 wt% epoxy/AlN nanocomposite has the best effect to suppress the space charge accumulation. For epoxy/AlN nanocomposites, both the electric stress reduction effect and electrical properties should be considered.

title: Clustering News on Iran's Nuclear Negotiations Using K-Means
authors: Amir Hossein Baradaran, Mohammad Ali Keyvanrad
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The nuclear negotiations between Iran and world powers have been a focal point of international attention and political discourse for over two decades. Understanding how media outlets cover and frame these events is essential for analyzing public perception and geopolitical narratives. Despite the importance of this issue, no previous research has focused on automatically clustering news related to Iran’s nuclear negotiations. This study employs the K-Means clustering algorithm to group Persian-language news articles based on thematic similarity. The resulting clusters reveal distinct patterns in domestic media coverage, shedding light on dominant narratives and shifts in focus over time.

title: Research on Power Prediction and Optimal Scheduling of Wind-Solar-Storage Systems for Industrial Parks
authors: Shuai Wu, Zijun Yang, Tianqing Li, Jie Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To enhance economic efficiency and renewable energy accommodation in park-level wind-solar-storage hybrid systems, this paper proposes an integrated framework combining high-precision forecasting and optimal scheduling. A CNN-LSTM hybrid model is developed for accurate short-term wind and solar power forecasting. Based on the forecast results, a multi-objective optimization scheduling model — considering energy storage operational constraints — is formulated and solved using an improved particle swarm optimization (PSO) algorithm to minimize external electricity purchase costs while maximizing renewable energy utilization. Simulations for a typical industrial park under representative summer and winter weeks, covering both high- and low-output renewable scenarios, validate the proposed strategy. It reduces external power purchase costs to ¥348,900 and achieves a 95.27% renewable energy utilization rate, with effective peak shaving, valley filling, and storage of surplus photovoltaic generation. This work provides practical models and methods for optimized operation of industrial park energy systems, offering valuable guidance for renewable integration and cost reduction.

title: Analytical Determination of Fractional Orders in FOPID Controllers Based on Closed-Loop Dynamics
authors: Ion Fiodorov, Bartolomeu Izvoreanu, Irina Cojuhari, Dumitru Moraru, Nadejda Palamarciuc
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: The paper presents an analytical method for determining the fractional orders of the integral and derivative components of the fractional PID controller. The approach is based on the analysis of the dominant pole pair dynamics of the closed loop system, characterized by the natural frequency and the damping ratio, as well as on the influence of these parameters on the fractional orders of the integral and derivative components of the controller. Based on this analysis, explicit analytical relations are proposed, in which the fractional orders are expressed as functions of the normalized deviations of the natural frequency and damping ratio from reference values, weighted by sensitivity coefficients that quantify the influence of the dynamic parameters of the system. A synthesis algorithm is presented in which, starting from an integer-order PID controller tuned by a suitable tuning method, the dynamic parameters of the closed loop system are identified and used to compute the fractional orders of the integral and derivative components. The proposed methodology reduces the dependence on numerical optimization procedures or heuristic tuning and provides a transparent and reproducible framework for the design of fractional controllers according to the desired closed loop dynamics. The effectiveness of the proposed method is demonstrated through two case studies.

title: Exploring the Use of Chatbots for Inclusion and Diversity in Higher Education
authors: Rosa Maria Vasconcelos, Emlia Araújo, Inês Amaral, Inês Castro, Bárbara Almeida
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: This working in progress paper debates how artificial intelligence (AI) can support inclusive teaching and learning practices in STEM, with a specific focus on engineering education at the postgraduate level. Building on a synthesis of recent studies on AI-enabled learning, inclusion, and accessibility in higher education, the paper identifies current gaps in how AI tools are conceptualised for diverse doctoral and master's student populations. It then presents examples of educational chatbots designed for academic and student support and discusses how these tools can be aligned with the principles of Universal Design for Learning (UDL). Particular attention is given to inclusion variables-such as age, full-time or part-time enrolment, nationality and language background, gender, and disability-related requirements-and to how personalised conversational pathways may enhance equitable participation and academic success.

title: Coordinated Optimization of Distribution Network and Hydrogen-based Integrated Energy System Based on Stackelberg Game Theory under Carbon-Green Certificate Trading Interaction Mechanism
authors: Yongqiang Tao, Shangpeng Zhong
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address issues such as insufficient fairness in carbon emission accounting and the complexity of coordinating interests among multiple stakeholders in energy market, this paper proposes a coordinated optimization method for distribution network and hydrogen-based integrated energy system (HIES) under a carbon-green certificate trading interaction mechanism based on Stackelberg game theory. First of all, a carbon emission accounting model based on power flow tracing and green certificate subscription is constructed to achieve precise and equitable measurement of electricity users' carbon emissions. Secondly, an interaction mechanism between carbon trading and green certificate trading is designed to promote carbon asset interoperability and green consumption. Furthermore, an optimization model based on Stackelberg game theory is established with the distribution network as the leader and the HIES as the follower, guiding the system toward economic and low-carbon operation through dynamic electricity price. Case study based on the IEEE 33-node system demonstrate that the proposed method achieves accurate and equitable carbon accounting while coordinating the optimization of multiple stakeholders' interests. This paper lays a strategic foundation for enhancing renewable energy integration rates and reducing system carbon emissions.

title: Study on the Rationality of Water Supply, Drainage and Fire Protection Pipeline Layout in Integrated Pipe Gallery of Nuclear Power Plant
authors: Shuo Cui, He Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: In the integrated pipe gallery of nuclear power plants, water supply, drainage, and fire protection pipelines often exhibit a situation of "multiple systems co-located in the same gallery with strong constraints and coupling." This situation can lead to spatial conflicts, insufficient hydraulic margins, and risks associated with multiple disasters. Therefore, a framework for rational evaluation and improvement of the layout was created, relying on parametric BIM and scenario-driven hydraulic analysis. This study uses zoning codes and interface dictionaries as the basis for unified data standards, creating calculable constraints such as clearance, passageways, and zoning crossings, thereby forming a boundary condition library for normal operation, maintenance switching, single pump failure, fire, extreme inflow, and coupled operating conditions. By using conflict thermal judgment, pressure margin distribution, and pressure drop contribution decomposition, corridors with severe conflicts and key bottlenecks are identified. Subsequently, fire water demand, water accumulation dynamics, and seismic utilization of supports and hangers are integrated into a unified KPI system for multi-objective scheme comparison and selection, resulting in a verifiable recommended layout scheme and engineering dataset.

title: Public Transport Integration of Regional Capitals in the Czech Republic
authors: Lukáš Kacar, Milan Sliacky
publish: 2026 Smart City Symposium Prague (SCSP)
abstract: The article deals with the issue of integrating public transport of regional capitals into integrated regional public transport systems. The aim of the work is to analyze the degree and forms of fare integration in thirteen regional cities, focusing on the conditions for short-term single fares, which best reflect the actual interconnection of urban and regional transport from the passenger's point of view. Based on four predefined criteria of the proposed methodology, individual cities are compared in terms of acceptation of state discounts, the existence of parallel public transport fares, the price differentiation between urban and regional zones, and differences between the use of urban and regional lines within the urban area. The results show considerable heterogeneity in the approaches of individual regions, which is conditioned by institutional arrangements, tariff policy, and the technical capabilities of ticketing systems. Plzeň, Liberec, Jihlava, and Ústí nad Labem show the highest degree of integration, while in Ostrava and Olomouc there are still significant tariff differences between urban and regional transport. Prague and Brno achieve a medium level of integration, with their extensive public transport systems posing a specific challenge in terms of financing social discounts. The study also confirms the tendency between the size of a regional capital and the level of integration of city public transport into integrated public transport systems (IPTS) and points to the influence of the fare model used. The article emphasizes that full fare and operational integration brings significant benefits to passengers but requires intensive coordination between cities, regions, and their transport organizers. The conclusions of the study can help in the integration process of public transport in the Czech Republic. If the initial assumptions are met, these conclusions may also be applied abroad.

title: AI-Driven Battlefield Decision Support Using Probabilistic Threat Modeling (Bayesian Networks) Integrated with Multi-Objective Optimization (NSGA-II)
authors: Joe Prathap P M, Thanushree C Somapur, W Vinil Dani
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: Modern battlefield environments are characterized by high uncertainty, dynamic threat evolution, and the need for rapid, data-driven decision-making. Traditional rule-based or deterministic decision-support systems often fail to handle incomplete or ambiguous information, leading to suboptimal mission outcomes. To address this, the present work proposes an AI-driven battlefield decision support framework that integrates probabilistic threat modeling using Bayesian Networks with multi-objective optimization via NSGA-II (Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II). The Bayesian Network module estimates realtime threat probabilities by reasoning over uncertain sensor, intelligence, and situational data, providing a probabilistic situational awareness layer. These probabilistic outputs are then used by the NSGA-II optimizer to generate and evaluate tactical decisions based on multiple conflicting objectives—such as maximizing mission success probability, minimizing response time, and reducing resource utilization. The proposed hybrid model is validated through a simulated battlefield scenario using synthetic datasets representing diverse threat conditions and resource constraints. Experimental results demonstrate that the integrated Bayesian–NSGA-II approach yields robust, adaptive, and explainable decision recommendations compared to traditional deterministic systems. This research highlights the potential of combining probabilistic reasoning and evolutionary optimization to enhance real-time decision-making, resource planning, and operational resilience in defense applications.

title: A Miniaturized Frequency Reconfigurable Antenna for Sub-6 GHz 5G and WBAN Communications
authors: Vignesh M, Arulmurugan S, Minakshmi Shaw, Pradeeksha P V, Sobika A K, Sarveshwaran J
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: A frequency reconfigurable antenna operateing at 3.3 GHz and 5.8 GHz is investigated using RF PIN diodes. The antenna consists of two rectangular patches divided by a narrow gap. This configuration enables frequency switching via PIN diode bias control. Depending on the diode biasing the antenna can switch efficiently between 3.3 GHz 5 G communication and 5.8 GHz, for WBAN communication. Simulation outcomes validate performance throughout the switching operation. The reconfigurable antenna provides a peak gain of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2. 4 2 \unicode{x007E} d B i}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 3}$</tex> GHz (ON State). 6.98 dBi at 5.8 GHz (OFF state). It offers impedance bandwidth of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 7 \%}$</tex> at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{3. 3 \sim G H z}$</tex> in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{O N}$</tex> mode (155 MHz) and 3.31 % at 5.8 GHz, in the OFF mode (192 MHz) demonstrating consistent bandwidth performance in both states. This design suits wireless communication systems, particularly Wireless Body Area Networks (WBAN) and sub-6 GHz 5G implementations. These results show stable radiation characteristics with acceptable gain at both frequencies.

title: Impact on the Online Educational Experience of Learning Platforms and Their Contribution to the Teaching Process
authors: Mateo Vargas-Chicota, Alicia Cedeño-Tello, Joe Llerena-Izquierdo
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: The objective of this research is to evaluate the level of satisfaction and academic performance associated with the use of the Moodle LMS in 525 students from May to September 2024 of the Basic and Initial Education courses at the Universidad Politécnica Salesiana, Guayaquil. An empiricalanalytical approach with a quantitative approach was used, through structured and statistically processed surveys. The results show that accessibility to materials <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(47 \%)$</tex>, evaluation tools (43 %) and additional ICT resources (41 %) were the most valued aspects. 46 % of the respondents consider Moodle to be very effective for accessing content, and 50 % perceive an improvement in their academic performance. It is concluded that digital platforms are valuable tools, but their positive impact depends on strengthening infrastructure, teacher training and improving educational interaction.

title: Low-Carbon Economic Dispatch of Integrated Energy System Considering Green Hydrogen Certificates and Carbon Quotas for New Energy Vehicles
authors: Xueyan Liu, Junze Hu, Jinxuan Li, Jingwen Wang, Luyao Pi, Xiusong Xue
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Integrated energy systems (IES) are important carriers for achieving carbon neutrality targets. This paper proposes an economic dispatch strategy for IES. This strategy includes multiple low-carbon incentives and a refined two-stage power-to-gas (P2G) model. On the source side, we introduce a green hydrogen certificate trading (GHCT) mechanism. This mechanism quantifies the environmental premium of green hydrogen. On the network side, we construct a two-stage P2G operation model. This model includes an electrolyzer (EL), a methane reactor (MR), and a hydrogen fuel cell (HFC). It broadens the flexible conversion paths of hydrogen energy. On the load side, we propose a carbon quota trading mechanism for new energy vehicles (NEVs). This guides the low-carbon response and builds a low-carbon closed loop among the source, network, and load. Based on this, we build a mixed-integer linear programming optimization model. The objective is to minimize the total operating cost of the system. Simulation results show that the proposed dispatch strategy effectively improves the participation enthusiasm of NEV owners. It also enhances the economic and low-carbon performance of system operation.

title: A Fuzzy C-Means Approach to Classify Learning Styles for Educational Analysis
authors: Charles Fernando Velázquez Dodge, Sandra Elizabeth León Sosa, Ernesto Alfonso Larrieta Hernandez
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: The Felder-Silverman model is widely used in education to characterize students' learning preferences; thus, educators recognize that learners do not follow a single learning style. While the model defines four discrete dimensions of learning styles [12], [13], students often do not fit neatly into a single category along each dimension. This does not fully represent the complexity of a learner, and the authors suggest not labeling a learner in this form. To provide a more nuanced understanding of learners, this work proposes a novel method based on the Felder-Silverman model that emphasizes educational data analysis rather than instructional outcomes. The results include a comparison between discrete classical styles and the FCLS (Fuzzy Clustering Learning Styles). The fuzzy information provides a richer student description for exploratory educational analysis. Compared to other fuzzy methods [5]–[7], we combine group clustering with information fuzzification to convert four discrete dimensions into eight dimensions with continuous values using the original questionnaire. The results demonstrate the applicability of the proposed method at various educational levels, including elementary, high school, and university.

title: Breaking Barriers: Gender Balance Achievements in Energy Transition Education Across Latin America the EU-BEGP Multi-Cultural Experience
authors: Ricardo E. Bianchi, Agatha Clarice da Silva-Ovando
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: The European Union co-funded project “Modernising Digital Education in Energy Transition for Circular Economy in Latin America” (EU-BEGP, 2023-2026) brings together seven countries and eleven institutions to rethink how gender balance in STEM education can be achieved. This paper presents how the consortium has advanced women's participation, paying attention to the regional and cultural factors that shape opportunities in energy transition fields. Data from Bolivia, Ecuador, Guatemala, Perú, Spain, and France reveal encouraging progress: women now account for 44 % of participants, a figure that challenges the male-dominated traditions of STEM. The results also show that some institutions have been especially effective in engaging women. These findings suggest that broader results can be achieved when gender-inclusive strategies are intentional and reinforced through international collaboration. By highlighting how different cultural contexts influence participation, EU-BEGP offers practical lessons for educators and policymakers committed to building more diverse and inclusive STEM programs.

title: Research on Charging Load Clustering and Forecasting Based on Electric Vehicle Behavioral Profiles
authors: Yong Xiao, Fan Zhang, Xiaoming Lin, Ji Wang, Bin Qian, Jianlin Tang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Electric vehicle (EV) data are characterized by real-time and dynamic attributes, but their collection and processing face two major challenges. First, privacy concerns restrict access to detailed data. Second, the massive volume of data leads to high time and labor costs in processing. To address these issues, this paper proposes an EV behavioral profiling method based on mutual information feature selection, K-means clustering, and Monte Carlo simulation. By using charging behavior profiles, the proposed method enables efficient and secure description and transfer of EV data. In the feature selection stage, the mutual information method is adopted to reduce data dimensionality and complexity while preserving data representativeness. Then, EV charging behaviors are classified into four categories through K-means clustering, and their characteristic variables are fitted with Gaussian distributions to construct EV behavioral profiles. Finally, Monte Carlo-based load forecasting is carried out based on these profiles. Case studies show that the proposed method can effectively predict the charging load curve in a certain area, thereby providing support for system planning and dispatching..

title: Multi-Time-Scale Baseline Calculation Method for Industrial and Commercial Users
authors: JingYi Zhang, YongBiao Yang, Jiao Du, ZhenJun Lu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the expansion of renewable power generation, power system loads have become more volatile and uncertain, making demand response a key technology for new-type power system construction. As key targets for load flexibility, industrial and commercial users’ Customer Baseline Load calculation is fundamental to incentive-based demand response. However, traditional methods have low historical data utilization, are vulnerable to users’ fraudulent load-raising, and lack accuracy. To address these issues, this paper proposes a multi-time-scale baseline calculation method based on two-stage clustering. Experimental validation shows the method offers significantly improved accuracy and anti-disturbance capability compared to the traditional national standard average method, providing a reliable basis for load regulation calculation and incentive subsidy issuance in demand response.

title: Time Series Foundation Model for Electricity Load Forecasting
authors: Wenteng Kuang, Qipei Zhang, Bo Yang, Weichao Jiang, Han Li, Yize Yang, Yuanzheng Hu, Bin Han
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the high-penetration integration of renewable energy and the growth of diverse flexible loads, the operational characteristics of power systems have become increasingly complex, rendering traditional load forecasting methods inadequate for precise regulation requirements. Although time series foundation models have achieved breakthroughs in general domains, they still face three major challenges in electricity load forecasting scenarios: domain adaptation barriers, insufficient covariate modeling, and sample imbalance. To address these issues, this paper proposes a power-specific time series foundation model tailored for load forecasting applications. First, we constructed a pre-training dataset for power load covering multiple regions and temporal scales. Second, we designed a time series foundation model framework integrating temporal embeddings and multi-source covariate embeddings, innovatively introducing a type-aware variable attention mechanism to dynamically adjust the influence weights of different covariate types. Finally, we proposed a dynamic weighted sampling strategy incorporating temporal embeddings to effectively mitigate sample sparsity issues in special scenarios such as holidays. Experimental results demonstrate that the model achieves zero-shot prediction average accuracy exceeding 97% on a provincial power grid. After fine-tuning for scenarios including holidays and abnormal weather conditions, prediction accuracy significantly improved with an average accuracy increase of 3.63%. In field validation, the prediction accuracy during critical periods reached expert-level empirical standards. This research effectively enhances the intelligence level and robustness of Electricity load forecasting.

title: AI Ethics Must Speak the Language of Business
authors: Tim Weninger
publish: ACM AI Letters
abstract: None

title: Degradation-Aware Blind Light-Field Image Quality Assessment with Linear Attention
authors: Youzhi Zhang, Jianyu Qian, Deyang Liu, Xiaofei Zhou, Hongbin Zha, Caifeng Shan
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Blind Light-Field Image Quality Assessment (LFIQA) is challenging, as degradations are microlens-dependent and spatially non-uniform, while perceptual quality relies on both spatial fidelity and angular consistency. However, many existing methods either assume globally stationary distortions or adopt global pooling or self-attention, which can be biased by locally corrupted lenslets and become computationally prohibitive when modeling long-range spatial-angular dependencies. Therefore, in this letter, we present a degradation-aware framework that first predicts a microlens reliability map to make quality inference robust to spatially non-uniform, lenslet-varying corruption. It then extracts spatial and angular features, applies a shared Receptance Weighted Key Value (RWKV) module for linear-time long-range context, and fuses them to predict perceptual quality. Experiments show a higher correlation with subjective ratings with competitive efficiency.

title: DeRA-MOS: Optimizing Text-to-Music Evaluation via Decoupled Listwise Ranking and Modality Alignment
authors: Chien-Chun Wang, Hung-Shin Lee, Hsin-Min Wang, Berlin Chen
publish: IEEE Signal Processing Letters
abstract: Evaluating text-to-music (TTM) systems remains expensive because music impression (MI) and text alignment (TA) scores rely on human mean opinion scores (MOS). Most automatic MOS estimators are trained with point-wise regression or distributional classification. These objectives do not directly optimize rank-based metrics and provide weak geometric constraints for cross-modal coherence. To address these gaps, we propose DeRA-MOS, a decoupled optimization framework for TTM evaluation. For MI, we introduce a batch-aware listwise ranking loss that models relative order within each mini-batch and better aligns with evaluation based on Spearman's rank correlation coefficient (SRCC). For TA, we introduce a score-anchored modality alignment loss that maps human scores to target audio-text similarity and regularizes the latent space before fusion. By effectively mitigating the point-wise training mismatch and modality drift, experiments on MusicEval demonstrate that our decoupled framework yields substantial improvements in both MI and TA ranking metrics, establishing a robust paradigm for large-scale TTM evaluation.

title: FastStair: Learning to Run up Stairs With Humanoid Robots_supp1-3701575.mp4
authors: Yan Liu
publish: N/A
abstract: Running up stairs is effortless for humans but remains extremely challenging for humanoid robots due to the simultaneous requirements of high agility and strict stability. Model-free reinforcement learning (RL) can generate dynamic locomotion, yet implicit stability rewards and heavy reliance on task-specific reward shaping tend to result in unsafe behaviors, especially on stairs; conversely, model-based foothold planners encode contact feasibility and stability structure, but enforcing their hard constraints often induces conservative motion that limits speed. We present FastStair, a planner-guided, multi-stage learning framework that reconciles these complementary strengths to achieve fast and stable stair ascent. FastStair integrates a parallel model-based foothold planner into the RL training loop to bias exploration toward dynamically feasible contacts and to pretrain a safety-focused base policy. To mitigate planner-induced conservatism and the discrepancy between low- and high-speed action distributions, the base policy was fine-tuned into speed-specialized experts. These experts are retained as separate rule-switched branches, each equipped with independent LoRA layers that are co-fine-tuned to smooth the switching transition, yielding a single deployable controller that operates reliably across the full commanded-speed range. We deploy the resulting controller on the Oli humanoid robot, achieving stable stair ascent at commanded speeds up to 1.65 m/s and traversing a 33-step spiral staircase (17 cm rise per step) in 12 s, demonstrating robust high-speed performance on long staircases. Project Page: <uri xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">https://npcliu.github.io/FastStair</uri>.

title: RAG-Enhanced DistilBERT Model for Contextual Understanding of Polysemy in Digital News Titles
authors: Anusha, K Manjula Shenoy, Smitha N Pai
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: Digital news titles often include polysemous phrases to enhance linguistic efficiency. It offers major disambiguation challenges for computational natural language processing systems in media analysis and information retrieval applications. Large language models (LLMs) are better at reasoning in context, but they struggle with polysemy in news titles. These limitations are connected to the knowledge requirements that fail to account for the ongoing evolution of news-reporting language, as well as the inability to correct errors when headline styles are inconsistent. Also, constraints in contextual understanding of present developments and a greater rely on language. Computing devices have limited efficiency in real-time news processing. This study introduces a retrieval-augmented generation method to improve the management of polysemous terms in digital news. The suggested method adeptly handles the challenges identified by LLM through the use of external information sources, journalism databases, and contextual knowledge repositories. Understanding massive language models for digital news by using adaptive retrieval techniques enables accurate disambiguation of polysemous titles through integrated knowledge processes. The suggested research indicates that retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) enhanced frameworks can effectively address the challenges faced by LLMs in news-related tasks, demonstrating their performance in automated journalism analysis, headline-generating systems, and news categorization and summarization applications. The proposed RAG-DistilBERT model achieves 11.7% improvement in accuracy over standard models for the contextual interpretation of digital news titles.
