title: Dual-Mode Linear Cross-Polarization Converter-Based Highly Sensitive Refractive Index Sensor
authors: Pancham Kumar, Vikram Maurya, Sarthak Singhal
publish: IEEE Transactions on Plasma Science
abstract: A multiarm Z-shaped resonator and slot-based wideband dual-mode terahertz (THz) linear cross-polarization converter (LCPC) for refractive index-based biosensing applications is proposed. It has a unitcell volume of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.134\lambda _{\mathrm {L}} \times 0.093\lambda _{\mathrm {L}} \times 0.061\lambda _{\mathrm {L}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lambda _{\mathrm {L}} =148.5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m) while exhibiting reflective polarization conversion ratio (PCR<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {rx}}) \ge 80$</tex-math> </inline-formula>% across 2.02–6.59 THz and transmissive polarization conversion ratio (PCR<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {tx}}) \ge 80$</tex-math> </inline-formula>% across the spectrum of 3.67–3.91 THz and 6.43–7.29 THz. Physical phenomenon behind the polarization conversions is studied through electric field distribution. It has stable PCR<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {rx}} \ge 80$</tex-math> </inline-formula>% performance for incidence angles up to 30°. Its maximal sensitivity (S<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\max }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) as a refractive index sensor is 618 GHz/RIU using PCR<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rx</sub> and 792 GHz/RIU using PCR<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">tx</sub>. Its biosensor configuration has a S<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">max</sub> of 4.95 THz/RIU (PCR<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {tx}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) and 16.76 THz/RIU (PCR<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\mathrm {rx}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>). The proposed polarization converter’s wideband performance, dual-mode sensing capability, high sensitivity, and compact dimensions indicate that it is suitable for a variety of applications, such as wireless communication, biosensing, imaging, and security screening.

title: On the Business Mode of Clean Energy Bases Considering Water Energy Storage
authors: Jiansheng Zhou, Sujie Qiao, Shunli Jing, Jie Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address the challenges of integrating a high proportion of new energy sources into the grid, specifically curtailment and system stability issues, this paper proposes the concept of transforming and upgrading traditional hydropower stations into a "Water Energy Storage" (WES) system. This model leverages existing cascade reservoirs and constructs large-scale, long-duration, low-cost energy storage resources through engineering measures such as adding pumping stations, pump-turbines, or hydropower capacity expansion. Focusing on the Business Mode for WES-based clean energy bases, this paper systematically explores two primary investment and operation modes: independent and integrated. The research analyzes three technical pathways – pumping stations, pump-turbines, and hydropower expansion – examining their respective beneficiary entities, revenue composition (including energy fees, capacity fees, and ancillary service revenues), and corresponding price formation mechanisms (such as two-part tariffs and integrated feed-in tariffs). The results indicate that selecting an appropriate Business Mode coupled with a supportive pricing mechanism is crucial for unlocking the comprehensive benefits of WES and ensuring project economic viability. This study can provide decision-making references for the sustainable development of clean energy bases in China.

title: Generative AI-Powered Document Analysis with Large Language Models in the Semiconductor Sector
authors: Susan Sun, Melissa Gledhill, Ikhlas Manzur, Lional Ananda Wijethilaka, Siddharth Ajit, Abe Hsu, Jeff Shelman, Justin Chown
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: The field of Artificial Intelligence has witnessed a monumental shift with the emergence of Generative AI, and at its heart lie the powerful Large Language Models (LLMs). In this paper, we delve into LLMs and their application to document analysis in the semiconductor industry to enhance efficiency, effectiveness, and insight generation. We aim to highlight both the transformative capabilities and current limitations, emphasizing a balanced perspective for responsible and effective adoption of LLMs.

title: Dynamic Reprogrammable Photomask for Scalable Direct-Write Lithography
authors: Richard Beaudry, Raphaël Dawant, Md Iftekharul Islam, Amrid Amnache, Maurice Delafosse, Luc Fréchette, Serge Ecoffey
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Photolithography is the workhorse of semiconductor micro- and nanofabrication. Nevertheless, its flexibility is limited, since any modification of the design necessitates the fabrication of a new photomask. This work proposes a new approach: a dynamic reprogrammable photomask (DRP) in the shape and size of a standard photomask to fit in steppers or scanners. Hence, providing direct writing capability using existing tools. This work demonstrates direct writing capability with resolution up to 456 nm. This can lead to a competitive solution for routing pattern adaptation for high-density chiplet-to-chiplet interconnects even with shifted chiplets, for fast and numerous design iterations, and for die-level unique identification for supply chain traceability.

title: Mechanisms Underlying the Influence of Operational Factors in Flexible/Rigid-Mounted Photovoltaic Modules on Power Generation
authors: Baoyong Liu, Yi Yang, Xin Luan, Quan Liu, Jie Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Aiming at the lack of systematic comparative research on the power generation performance of flexible and rigid photovoltaic mounting systems, this paper takes a photovoltaic plant in Jinan as the research case to investigate the influence mechanism of three key operational factors: wind load, array spacing, and mounting height. A wind-vibration response analysis framework, a spacing-shading optimization model for three terrain conditions, and a thermal balance model are constructed, followed by quantitative analysis through PVsyst simulation and MATLAB calculation. On this basis, the power generation difference between the two systems is clarified, which provides theoretical support for the selection of photovoltaic mounting systems under different geographical conditions.

title: Optimized Scheduling of Green Electricity and Green Ammonia under High Wind and Solar Penetration
authors: Miaoyi Liu, Yongliang Liang, Wei Cong, Zhexuan Shuai, Fangyuan Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The penetration rate of renewable energy continues to rise, but its inherent randomness and volatility pose significant challenges to system operation. To achieve flexible operation of green ammonia plants under high renewable energy penetration, this study proposes a low-carbon economic dispatch method incorporating distributionally robust chance constraints. Initially, a green electricity-green ammonia system model is designed as the foundation of this research. Subsequently, the Wasserstein distance is employed to construct ambiguity sets from historical data, describing potential realizations of uncertain demand probability distributions. The deterministic model is reformed into a DRCC-optimized model, converted into a solvable mixed-integer nonlinear programming model. Tests on the IEEE 33-bus system show adjustable parameters balance economy and robustness, achieving above 84.51% DG penetration within a network loss below 5.64%.

title: AI-Driven Die-level Defect Disposition
authors: Troy Acuff, Anurima Dey, Praveen Kumar Suthakar, Ting Wu, Scott Garner, Viswa Teja Polali
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper describes an AI-driven wafer disposition method that uses Mask R-CNN instance segmentation with automated guard-banding to handle complex, irregular defect patterns that are poorly screened by wafer-averaged limits and often require subjective manual map review. The model localizes and classifies spatial patterns, then projects masks to the die grid for die-level risk attribution. Adjacent risk is captured using the Good-Die–Bad-Neighborhood 3×3 matrix rule (GDBN9), expanding from defect "seed" dies to neighbors. Reliability is validated with burn-in hazard-curve analysis: wafers in the AI-segmented BAD cohort show 1.56× higher end-of-stress failure-in-time (FIT) than matched GOOD controls. The approach reduces manual intervention and enables consistent, reliability-aligned automation of disposition decisions.

title: Performance Evaluation of Alternative Grading Practices for Sustainable Undergraduate Electrical Engineering Education
authors: Alex Mouapi
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: Alternative grading practices (AGPs) are increasingly popular in undergraduate education, but research on the outcomes of these strategies in terms of skill acquisition and validation is limited. AGP aim to motivate students with learning difficulties by avoiding feedback accompanied by a grade. In this study, the scores achieved in AGP are compared with those achieved using the Standard Evaluation Method (SEM). The subjects of this study were 52 junior students in Electrical Engineering at the Polytechnic School of Montreal. The subjects were initially submitted to the AGPs, then the same students were placed in the context of SEM. Analyses of the results in the context of AGP and SEM are initially performed, and it emerges that the grades obtained in the context of AGP overestimate the students' levels. Compared to the scores achieved in AGP, the average grade of the group is higher than that achieved in SEM. However, the highest grades were not necessarily achieved during AGP. Overall, it appears that the AGPs help to improve the group average grade but not necessarily to ensure that all students achieve the expected competencies. Expected grades, study failure rates, and student satisfaction are also analyzed, along with avenues for future research regarding skills acquisition, validation, and transfer in engineering education.

title: A Unified Virtual Metrology Modeling Framework for Process Consistency and Rapid Deployment in Multi-Chamber Manufacturing
authors: Yen-Chun Lo
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a multi-chamber Virtual Metrology (VM) modeling framework designed to enhance predictive consistency and scalability across multiple chambers in semiconductor manufacturing. Unlike conventional single-chamber VM systems that require isolated model training and maintenance, the proposed framework integrates chamber variability analysis and encoding-based unified modeling to create a single, transferable model that captures both shared and chamber-specific process characteristics. By embedding tool-to-tool variation (TTV) assessment directly into the modeling workflow, the framework enables efficient tool matching, rapid deployment to new chambers, and consistent quality prediction, ultimately reducing engineering effort and improving overall fab productivity.

title: Enhanced PCG Denoising Using a Multi-Stage Processing Chain and MLP-Driven VMD IMF Optimization
authors: Sobhan Rezazadeh, Mohammad Javad Ghoreishian, Hosna Ghofrani, Alireza Zaherfekr, Seyedfarzad Jalali, Farideh Ebrahimi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach for enhancing PCG signal quality by integrating a signal-processing chain with neural-network–based IMF minimization. The proposed pipeline includes low-pass filtering, median filtering, and adaptive thresholding to suppress noise and reveal heart-sound components, followed by Variational Mode Decomposition (VMD) for time–frequency analysis. A key contribution of this work is determining the minimum number of IMFs required to accurately represent PCG dynamics, which is crucial for reducing computational cost, avoiding mode redundancy, and improving the interpretability of decomposed components. To achieve this, a multilayer perceptron (MLP) network is trained using features extracted from each segmented cardiac cycle, enabling binary classification of IMF requirements. The model achieves high accuracy in distinguishing whether a signal requires 6 or 10 IMFs. Simulation results across 31 patient recordings demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves heart-sound clarity and efficiency. This framework provides a reliable and scalable solution for automated PCG analysis in clinical and wearable-device applications.

title: Architectural Design of a Power System Reliability Information Management System Based on a Control Cloud Platform
authors: Shiji Wang, Yunting Song, Ziqi Wang, Qian Wang
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: In response to the emerging challenges posed by traditional and new stability issues in the "dual-high" new power system with a high proportion of renewable energy and high penetration of power electronics, as well as the insufficient adaptability of existing reliability management architectures, this paper proposes a power system reliability information management system architecture based on a control cloud platform. Different from conventional reliability management systems that mainly focus on post-event statistics and lack cross-level collaboration, a whole-process reliability assessment framework covering pre-event prediction, in-process evaluation, and post-event review is constructed to unify the adequacy and security dimensions of power system reliability. Guided by the control cloud architecture, a three-level dispatching framework (national–regional–provincial) is designed to realize cross-level data interaction and service scheduling through the control cloud service bus. Three core modules, namely data source repository, index calculation and analysis, and data reporting, are developed to support automatic multi-source data aggregation, full-process online index computation, and multi-level collaborative management. Compared with existing systems, the proposed architecture features better cloud-native adaptability, higher automation, and stronger coordination capability, providing effective technical support for reliability management in the new power system.

title: A Multi-Scale Wavelet Based Deep Learning Architecture for Effective EEG Artifact Removal
authors: Reza Hadi-Sadegh, Mohammadreza Hassannejad Bibalan
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: This paper proposes Wave-U-Net3+, a novel framework that integrates wavelet packet decomposition (WPD) with an enhanced U-Net3+ backbone incorporating residual connections, inception blocks, and convolutional block attention modules (CBAMs). WPD provides a comprehensive multi-resolution time–frequency representation of EEG signals, while the backbone learns intrinsic features of artifact-contaminated data for effective reconstruction and mixed noise suppression. The results highlight the efficacy of combining advanced signal processing techniques with modern deep learning architectures for robust and high-fidelity EEG artifact and mixed noise removal.

title: Implementing amine-based cleaning chemical for post CMP cleaning of Cu for BEOL interconnect and Hybrid bonding Applications
authors: Arunkumar G V, Wei-Tsu Tseng, Jeffrey Lang, Emiko Motoyama, Donald Canaperi, Govind Bajpai
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: The current contribution presents a study on improving the device yield and reliability by implementing an advanced post CMP clean chemical at the back-end-of-the-line for Cu and liner materials for interconnect applications. In this study, cleaning performance was evaluated for various applications including Cu thin wires at 2nm technology nodes, Cu fat wires, and TSVs for hybrid bonding. The traditional TMAH-based post-CMP clean chemical used in the past 2 decades poses an adverse environmental effect and causes EHS and handling issues in the high-volume manufacturing environment. This study demonstrates the implementation of a non TMAH-based post CMP cleaning chemical with superior cleaning efficiency that improves device yield for advanced nodes and mitigates EHS and chemical handing concerns.

title: Topology Derivation of Single-Stage Multi-Port Inverters Based on Reinforcement Learning
authors: Yuanxiang Meng, Shunfeng Yang, Zijian Ge, YongJiang Yu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Single-stage multiport inverters (SSMIs) have attracted increasing attention in multi-energy access and power conversion systems due to their reduced conversion stages, compact structure, and high power density. However, most existing SSMI topologies are derived from conventional multilevel inverters, which leads to relatively limited structural forms and makes it difficult to satisfy the future requirements of multi-source energy systems for greater topological diversity and flexibility. In addition, existing topology derivation methods largely rely on human expertise and predefined design rules, placing high demands on designers’ professional knowledge and limiting their scalability and automation. To address these issues, this paper proposes a reinforcement learning-based topology derivation method for SSMIS. By constructing a topology generation environment, the agent is guided to progressively search for feasible topologies through device connection and structural expansion, thereby enabling the automatic derivation of SSMI topologies. Using the proposed method, a series of topologies were generated, and one representative topology was further validated through simulation, demonstrating the feasibility of the proposed approach.

title: An AI-Driven Edge-Intelligent Framework for Protection, Operation, and Control of Distributed Generation in Smart Grids
authors: Megha Bhardwaj
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The rapid growth of distributed generation (DG) introduces operational and protection challenges in modern smart grids. This paper proposes an integrated AI-driven edge-intelligent framework combining adaptive protection, model predictive control (MPC), and multi-agent systems (MAS). A detailed mathematical formulation, control architecture, and simulation-based validation using the IEEE 33-bus system are presented. Results show significant improvements in voltage stability, fault detection accuracy, and energy efficiency.

title: Comparative Performance of CNN-based Models for Seizure Detection in EEG Signals with SMOTE Data Balancing
authors: Mahdieh Sadat Abdollahzadeh Daronkolaee, Seyedeh Somayeh Naghibi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Epilepsy is a long-term neurological condition that involves recurrent seizure episodes resulting from irregular brain activity. The present work proposes an electroencephalogram (EEG) analysis framework to automatically detect epileptic events, built upon data obtained from the Bonn dataset and focusing on three classes: ZO-NF-S. The recorded EEG signals are analyzed using the Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) to obtain sub-band representations, and Hjorth parameters are calculated from these bands as key distinguishing features. To achieve balanced class representation, the Synthetic Minority Over-Sampling Technique (SMOTE) is utilized during the training phase to generate additional minority class samples. The resulting features are classified using deep learning models. The main innovation of this work is combining SMOTE with the DWT-Hjorth feature pipeline and deep learning models to effectively address class imbalance in epileptic seizure detection on the Bonn dataset. Convolutional Neural Network-Bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory (CNN-BiLSTM) and Convolutional Neural Network-Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit (CNN-BiGRU) achieved the highest accuracies of 96.2%, while without SMOTE their accuracies were 95.6% and 95.2%, respectively. Overall, the results verify that the introduced approach strengthens the capability of detecting seizures and provides a reliable basis for automated epilepsy diagnosis.

title: Cross-Border Transfer Learning for 1-km Soil Moisture Downscaling in Romania
authors: Mihai-Bogdan Stan, Stefan-Gheorghe Pentiuc
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Accurate monitoring of soil moisture is a critical component for water resource management, but data from satellite missions such as NASA SMAP are limited by a coarse spatial resolution of 25–36 km. This data is too general for practical applications in Romania, such as precision agriculture or flood forecasting in mountainous areas. Conventional downscaling techniques often rely on optical imagery, which is frequently unusable in the Carpathian regions due to cloud cover. This paper proposes the TADA (Topographically-Aware Domain Adaptation) framework, which eliminates dependence on weather conditions by using static topographic predictors (altitude, slope, and aspect) extracted through Google Earth Engine. To overcome the limitation caused by the low density of the in-situ monitoring network in Romania, a cross-border Transfer Learning strategy called Super-Spiking was implemented. The model, using the LightGBM algorithm, was pre-trained on a high-density dataset from Germany (~11.7 million records) to learn the fundamental physical relationships between relief and humidity. The model was then fine-tuned using a local dataset from Romania (~420,000 records), representing only 3.6% of the source domain volume. Experimental results show that, while direct model transfer (Zero-Shot) suffers from high systematic error (bias of 0.085 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>), the Super-Spiking technique neutralizes these errors, achieving an accuracy of 0.037 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> (ubRMSE). This performance satisfies the SMAP Level-4 (L4) soil moisture accuracy requirement of ubRMSE < 0.04 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>.

title: Research on Optimal Configuration Scheme for Reactive Power Compensation Based on Typical Flexible Retrofitting Configuration of LCC-HVDC
authors: Yanqi Hou, Kuan Li, Yuchen Gan, Dazhou Zhou, Chenhao Li, Zhou Li
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address the issues of unsmooth reactive power compensation, frequent mechanical switching, and voltage fluctuations in Line-Commutated Converter High Voltage Direct Current (LCC-HVDC) systems during dynamic active power regulation, this paper proposes an optimal configuration scheme for reactive power compensation based on a typical flexible retrofitting configuration (parallel SVG). This scheme prioritizes the dynamic and continuous regulation advantages of the Static Var Generator (SVG) and combines them with the basic static support of capacitor banks to scientifically coordinate the reactive power output between continuous and discrete devices. Simulation results demonstrate that under continuous active power regulation scenarios, the proposed scheme achieves precise and smooth reactive power compensation, effectively suppresses AC voltage fluctuations, and significantly reduces the switching frequency of voltage regulation equipment, substantially enhancing the system's voltage stability and comprehensive economic efficiency.

title: A Computer Vision Framework for Context-Aware Adaptive Vehicular Visible Light Communications
authors: Mykhailo Lebid, Alin-Mihai Căilean, Cătălin Beguni, Eduard Zadobrischi, Sebastian-Andrei Avătămăniţei
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: Vehicular Visible Light Communication (VVLC) is a promising solution for communication-based vehicle safety applications that integrates existing transport infrastructure and Automotive Lighting Systems (ALS). Despite a high potential, its effectiveness is limited by channel dynamics, the need for line-of-sight (LoS), and the influence of external factors, including solar radiation and weather conditions such as rain, fog, and others, which cause scattering, absorption, and attenuation of the optical signal. Therefore, the development of adaptive systems capable of taking into account the environmental context is particularly relevant. Computer Vision (CV) methods offer a practical solution for extracting scene information without the use of additional specialized sensor equipment. In this paper, a VVLC context-aware framework based on CV methods is proposed as a solution, allowing for the extraction of environmental parameters and their use to adapt VLC receiver parameters. The approach is implemented using CV tools for scene analysis, segmentation, and temporal filtering within MATLAB simulations, enabling the extraction of feature vectors that support the VLC system.

title: Niche and Disruptive Technologies in Defence and Security
authors: Saurabh Sanwal, Manoj Kumar Yadav, Rajagopal Chellamani Srikanth
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: Modern Warfare is being transformed by niche and disruptive technologies - specialized, cutting-edge innovations that can render traditional systems obsolete[1] [2]. Across the globe, defence establishments are investing heavily in domains such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Autonomous Systems (Unmanned Vehicles and Robots), Quantum Technologies, Biotechnology, Hypersonic Weapons, Directed-Energy Weapons (Lasers/ Microwaves) and advanced materials. These emerging fields promise enhanced situational awareness, decision-speed and asymmetric advantages, but also pose new strategic challenges. This paper surveys the latest disruptive defence technologies worldwide and examines India's efforts to adopt them. Real-world examples are highlighted: for instance, U.S. forces trialed a 60 kW naval laser to shoot down a drone[3] and Ukraine's army attributes two-thirds of Russian losses to small tactical UAVs[4]. An Indian perspective is included: from India's DRDO labs and emergent unmanned units to hypersonic missile tests and space assets (e.g. 2024 ASAT test). Strategic implications are also discussed (e.g. arms race, deterrence, ethical concerns) and policy initiatives (NATO's tech strategy[2], India-Russia cooperation on “niche” systems[5]). The paper concludes by outlining a pragmatic roadmap for leveraging niche technologies to enhance defence preparedness, operational resilience and long-term deterrence in an increasingly contested and technology-driven security environment.

title: Tactile Screens for the Development of Critical Thinking Skills in Engineering Courses
authors: Fabiola Salas-Diaz, Armando Elizondo-Noriega, Elsa C. Olivas-Castellanos, Damiam Moctezuma-Enriquez, Jacqueline Forte-Celaya
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: This paper presents a theoretical model for using tactile screens as an active learning pedagogical device to develop critical thinking skills among first-year engineering students, which in turn manifests through the conceptualization of solutions by applying physics concepts, such as the laws of conservation of matter and energy. The model involves the development of an application that allows students to interact with the content of two courses (Application of Conservation Laws in Engineering Systems and Computational Modeling Applying Conservation Laws) using tactile screens with an interconnected interface at a private university in northwestern Mexico. By facilitating the comprehension of topics, the change of posture of students (from sitting to standing up), among other advantages, the model 1) intends to develop critical thinking skills through applied examples and, therefore, 2) generate a greater diversity of solutions.

title: Modelling Diagrams to Enable Experiential and Active Learning on Face-to-Face Components of Blended Learning. A Survey of Learner Preferences
authors: Arturo Molina-Cristóbal, Xiao-Feng Kenan Kok, Swayamdipta Bhaduri, David Alejandro Diaz-Romero
publish: 2026 IEEE Engineering Education World Conference (EDUNINE)
abstract: The online component in blended learning is well supported with sophisticated learning management systems. However, there is a need to develop and investigate how to implement engaging face-to-face (F2F) activities in a Blended Learning approach in higher education. The F2F component offers educators opportunities to innovate in teaching methods that integrate well with online learning. We have designed a teaching method for F2F sessions and implemented it in two courses of the mechanical engineering programmes at the Singapore Institute of Technology (SIT) and the University of Glasgow Singapore. In this paper, we propose using modelling diagrams to guide students in solving engineering problems and to connect theory with experimentation, enabling experiential and active learning. Our study aims to obtain student feedback through structured questionnaires to identify student engagement levels. We present findings from the questionnaire on the learners' preferences. In particular, our findings identify that the proposed method improves student engagement and enables learning.

title: SiBCN within wafer uniformity improvement for Low-k spacer dielectrics
authors: Jaana Rajachidambaram, John Gumpher, Khagendra Kumar Nath, Victoria Castro Aguilar, Ravi Dey, Justin Clements, Rajasekaran Arumuganainar, Bas Korevaar
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Fully Depleted Silicon-on-Insulator (FDSOI) technology combines high performance, low power and cost-effective planar architecture with outstanding end-product functionality. One of the challenges in FDSOI integration is to obtain a low gate to source drain capacitance for which a robust low-k spacer material is needed with excellent film uniformity. The within wafer (WIW) film uniformity of low-k spacer material, silicon boron carbon nitride (SiBCN), is very critical as SiBCN acts as a gate encapsulation layer which is transformed into a spacer by a plasma-enhanced dry-etch process. In this paper we studied how gas flow dynamics inside a furnace could positively impact low-k spacer SiBCN WIW film uniformity deposited using atomic layer deposition (ALD) technique. Specifically, higher N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> carrier gas flow along with dichlorosilane (DCS) process gas injection during the deposition step resulted in > 50% improvement in WIW thickness compared to lower N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> carrier gas flow.Film characterization using X-ray Photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS), wet etch rate using Hot Phos and Mercury (Hg) probe reveals no changes in film properties between films deposited at different N<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> gas flows. Full wafer map electrical results show a lower NFET overlap capacitance (Cov) for the film with improved WIW uniformity

title: Finite Element Analysis of Shear Wave Propagation in Transversely Isotropic Media
authors: Mariah Prado, Gutemberg Cardoso, Florencia Vargas, Antonio Carneiro, Theo Pavan, Javier Brum
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Shear wave elastography (SWE) is widely used for noninvasive characterization of soft tissue mechanical properties; however, its application to anisotropic tissue, such as skeletal muscle, remains challenging due to direction-dependent shear wave propagation. This study aims to investigate the influence of transverse anisotropy on shear wave propagation (SWP) using numerical simulations. Three-dimensional finite element models were implemented to simulate SWP in transversely isotropic (TI) media with predefined fiber orientation and progressively increasing mechanical stiffness. Shear waves were generated through a localized transient excitation, and displacement maps were analyzed in different planes. Phase and group velocities were estimated using spectral analysis and a time-of-flight approach. The results demonstrate clear anisotropic behavior, with higher velocities in the direction parallel to the fibers compared to the perpendicular direction. Angular analyses revealed elliptical velocity distribution consistent with theoretical predictions for TI. Numerical results showed strong agreement with the analytical model. These findings demonstrate that the proposed finite element simulations reliably reproduce the anisotropic SWP predicted by theoretical models and observed experimentally, establishing a consistent numerical framework for the validation and interpretation of SWE in TI tissues.

title: Battery-Health-Oriented Parameter Design of LLC Converter
authors: Yuefeng Liao, Jiarui Dong, Duo Yang, Haoran Lv, Huanhuan Cui
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper investigates battery-health risks in high-power EV fast charging. Higher switching frequencies in onboard DC-DC converters shrink magnetics and raise power density, but they also increase high-frequency ripple and harmonics that can accelerate degradation under high current. We propose an LLC resonant-converter parameter optimization framework subject to state-of-health (SOH) constraints. A unified converter-battery mechanistic model is developed by coupling the LLC resonant tank with a first-order battery equivalent-circuit model. To move beyond the common "loss scales with current ripple" assumption, we introduce the tank quality factor Q and the normalized frequency detuning |Δ|, and derive a corrected relationship showing that additional harmonic power scales with the combined term Q|Δ|. We define Q|Δ| as an engineering metric for ripple stress, enabling comparison of resonant designs and their SOH trends. A fast-charging prototype is built, and two designs with identical input conditions, transformer turns ratio, and control strategy-differing only in tank parameters—are tested on 18650 NMC cells over 20-80% SOC. Cycle-life results validate the model and confirm that Q|Δ| discriminates SOH outcomes, providing practical guidance for balancing efficiency and battery longevity.

title: A Blockchain-Assisted UAV Authentication Scheme for Secure Offloading in 5G-Fog IoV Networks
authors: Yashar Salami
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: The rapid growth of 5G technologies and the Internet of Vehicles (IoV) has transformed intelligent transportation systems. However, dynamic highway environments still face challenges in ensuring communication security during computation offloading. This paper presents a hybrid UAV–Blockchain framework within a fog–edge federated architecture, enabling secure and transparent data exchange through 5G connectivity. In this model, the UAV serves as an intelligent relay between fog and vehicular layers, improving coverage, while blockchain ensures data integrity and trust. Security analysis using the AVISPA framework and the Dolev–Yao model confirms resistance against major attacks, including MITM and replay attacks. The proposed scheme achieves strong security with balanced computational efficiency, fully satisfying SRI 1–SRI 20 security requirements and providing a scalable and reliable solution for secure offloading in 5G–IoV networks.

title: Comparative Analysis of Alpha Rhythm Detection During Resting-State EEG: Emotiv EPOC X vs. Mikroe EEG Click
authors: Juan C. Delgado-Torres, Daniel Cuevas-González, A. Méndez-Zorrilla, Z. Hameed, Juan P. García-Vázquez, O. E. Barreras, Martín Aarón Sánchez-Barajas, Eladio Altamira-Colado
publish: 2026 Global Medical Engineering Physics Exchanges/ Pan American Health Care Exchanges (GMEPE/PAHCE)
abstract: Electroencephalography is one of the fundamental tools used for analyzing brain activity. Currently, commercial devices with features such as wireless data transmission and local and cloud data storage are emerging. However, these devices have technical limitations and require monthly subscriptions to access their features. An alternative to this problem is to develop open-source hardware and software technologies that can be used to perform assessments similar to those provided by high-cost devices. Therefore, the objective of this study is to perform a comparative analysis of alpha rhythm block detection during resting-state EEG. The commercial equipment Emotiv EPOC X was used as the reference device, and the Mikroe EEG Click analog front-end together with an Arduino Mega and an HC-06 module was used as a low-cost alternative. The experiment consisted of a two-minute recording session in which two states were recorded, divided into one minute with eyes closed followed by one minute with eyes open. This work aims to validate an alternative tool for electroencephalography data acquisition that is accessible to educational and research institutions.

title: Principles of Object-Process Methodology for System Architecture Modeling in Complex Transport Systems
authors: Vladimir Kremlik, Miroslav Svitek, Vojtech Merunka, Jiri Kofranek
publish: 2026 Smart City Symposium Prague (SCSP)
abstract: The paper introduces Object–Process Methodology (OPM; ISO 19450) as a unifying approach for designing a modular functional architecture of Mobility Hubs. OPM integrates the structural dimension (objects, assets, roles) and the behavioural dimension (processes, information and energy flows, control) into a single, coherent model. The paper outlines the core principles of OPM and its bimodal representation: graphical Object–Process Diagrams (OPDs) are paired with an equivalent textual specification in Object–Process Language (OPL). This duality supports intelligible communication with non-technical experts, facilitates requirements validation, and enables early detection of errors such as incompleteness, contradictions, or missing links between functions and resources. The practical value of OPM is demonstrated through a simplified case of passenger rail transport within the Czech Republic, where the end-to-end passenger journey is hierarchically decomposed. The example illustrates how OPM integrates processes with the required objects and resources (e.g., the app, ticket, travel plan, station, train, signaling) within a single coherent OPD/OPL model, enabling systematic checks of linkage completeness, decision conditions, and cross-level consistency.

title: Hybrid Orthogonal CNN-ResNet50 with Grey Wolf Optimization for Malware Classification on Malnet
authors: Yogendra Chhetri, Deepika, Sukhvir Kaur
publish: 2026 International Conference on Air Defense and Security (ICADS)
abstract: The rapid increase in the number of different forms of malware has made it difficult to rely on signaturebased detection methods. The main contribution of this paper is a new hybrid model whereby the OCNN and ResNet50 are used in combination, optimised using GWO for feature weighting selection, and the classification is based on ensemble learning techniques. Our approach is tested on the MalNet image-based malware dataset, achieving better results than state-of-the-art methods. The proposed architecture obtains 99.2% for accuracy and robust class-wise studying performance. Based on extensive 5-fold cross-validation, in addition to comparison with previous research, we analytically verify the efficiency of our hybrid architecture for the detection & classification of malware families from visual presentations.

title: Machine Learning-Based Rapid Optimization of the Air Curtain Flow Rates in EFEMs
authors: Hye Kwang Kim, Tae Jun Yun, Seung Joon Oh, Jin Ho Park, In Keun Woo, Tai-Su Park, Jungmin Ha, Young Jin Lim
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: The flow straightener (FS), also known as a laminar air curtain, installed at the exterior of the Front Opening Unified Pod (FOUP) door, requires case-specific optimization due to the distinct airflow dynamics of each Equipment Front End Module (EFEM). In current EFEM installations, FS tuning is typically performed through repeated on-site adjustments based on engineering experience. To address this limitation, we developed a machine-learning-assisted optimization framework driven by artificial intelligence (AI) that achieves rapid convergence, even in the presence of deliberately introduced turbulent obstructions. The proposed approach significantly shortens installation and calibration time and is expected to support yield improvement by minimizing humidity ingress into the FOUP environment.

title: Automated Test Specification Revision Comparison: A Memory-Optimized Hybrid Fuzzy Matching Algorithm for Large-Scale Parameter Analysis
authors: Ann Ann Boo, Belinda Ng
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper presents a novel three-phase parameter matching framework designed to automate the comparison of product test specifications across multiple revisions. Addressing the challenge of modern semiconductor manufacturing where test parameters often exceed 10,000 entries with complex evolutionary patterns, the proposed framework fundamentally improves upon traditional fuzzy matching. We introduce a hierarchical algorithm starting with a 'Phase 0' perfect-match reservation mechanism. This performs exhaustive bilateral scanning to identify and reserve exact parameter name matches after normalization, preventing the cascade matching errors inherent in conventional similarity-based systems. Building upon this foundation, the framework employs Phase 1 high-confidence fuzzy matching (≥90% similarity threshold) utilizing the Ratcliff-Obershelp algorithm to identify near-identical parameters, followed by Phase 2 medium-confidence matching (≥70% threshold) with relaxed length difference tolerances to capture substantial naming convention changes. The system achieves remarkable computational efficiency through the strategic implementation of Least Recently Used (LRU) caching, dynamic batch processing (adapting to ~200–500 parameters per batch), and periodic garbage collection. This reduces memory overhead and accelerates processing time by 85-90% for ~10,000-parameter datasets, making it suitable for industrial-scale validation. Additionally, the system generates comprehensive comparison matrices differentiating between specification changes (limit modifications) and test number updates, with confidence-scored matches (High/Medium/Low). Experimental results demonstrate superior accuracy in parameter matching with >90% precision for high confidence matches, enabling manufacturing engineers to rapidly assess revision impacts on production yield and test system configurations.

title: A Hierarchical Time-Sequenced Control Strategy for Active Power Frequency Support from Offshore Wind Farms
authors: Jingzhe Hu, Jingyu Wang, Dongyuan Shi, Zhangqin Hu
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: To address the critical challenges of deficient active frequency regulation, risk of frequency secondary drop, and supply-demand mismatch of reserve capacity during grid integration of large-scale offshore wind power via VSC-HVDC, this paper proposes an active power frequency support control strategy based on hierarchical temporal coordination and intelligent optimization. First, a novel three-level progressive control architecture encompassing " fast support, deep support, and backup support" is innovatively constructed, with differentiated triggering mechanisms and reserve capacity allocation rules designed to achieve time-phased on-demand release of frequency regulation resources; Second, an adaptive active power signal generation method is proposed, wherein dynamic compensation is calculated collaboratively through frequency deviation and rate of change of frequency (RoCoF); Subsequently, a dynamic reserve allocation mechanism based on " offline optimization of intelligent algorithms - online mapping via Feedforward Neural Network (FNN)" is established to achieve millisecond-level output of optimal hierarchical schemes; Afterwards, a hierarchical integer programming and greedy solving algorithm for wind turbines is designed to ensure that the practical allocation satisfies real-world engineering requirements. Finally, verification is conducted under four typical scenarios—step load change, secondary disturbance, rotor speed recovery-induced frequency dip, and wind turbulence—in a modified three-machine nine-bus system. The proposed strategy improves the frequency nadir by 33.80% and reduces the secondary drop depth by 30.20% compared to the scheme without active support under 10MW-level load disturbances, demonstrating superior scenario adaptability and robustness. This study provides a novel technical pathway and engineering reference for frequency stability control of high-penetration offshore wind power grid-connected systems.

title: Coordinated Control Strategy of Wind Farm and Energy Storage Systems for Fast Frequency Support Considering Secondary Frequency Drop
authors: Yajun Zhang, Wei Bao, Weitao Yao, Jun Yan, Zhaoxin Du, Aiqiang Pan
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: With the increasing proportion of wind power in power systems, fast frequency response from wind turbines can effectively enhance frequency stability in high-penetration wind power systems. However, in current wind farms participating in grid frequency support, the switching among multiple control modes across different time stages can lead to a noticeable issue of secondary frequency drop. This paper proposes a coordinated control strategy of wind farms and energy storage systems (ESS) for fast frequency support, considering secondary frequency drop. The complete wind turbine frequency response process is divided into three stages and designed accordingly. In the first stage, additional power control is employed to achieve rapid frequency response from wind turbines. The second stage addresses the secondary frequency drop when wind turbines withdraw from frequency support by coordinating the output of energy storage systems for transient frequency support. Besides, during the smooth transition stage, a preset control is designed for ESS to achieve a smooth transition from the rotor speed recovery stage back to maximum power point tracking control. The case study is simulated and validated using an AC power system with a wind farm and ESS. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed control framework, and comparative studies confirm its superiority.

title: On the Introduction of Vertical GaN MOSFET in High-Voltage Synchronous Buck Converters
authors: Tudorel Leampăr, Dorin O. Neacșu
publish: 2026 International Conference on Development and Application Systems (DAS)
abstract: This paper discusses the newly released vertical GaN MOSFET and provides a comparative assessment of reliability between Si, SiC, Lateral and Vertical GaN MOSFETs for a synchronous buck converter working with input voltage of 400 V/800 V and an output of 48 V at 10 A/5 A load current. Benefits of the Vertical GaN devices are presented together with a reliability comparison with the other current technologies. PSIM simulator is used to assess the power loss and temperatures of selected devices. The reliability and failure rates are calculated following the MIL-HDBK-217F (notice 2 of February 28th, 1995) procedure.

title: Enhancing Static Analysis through LLM-Based Semantic Validation
authors: Seyed Mohammad Taghavi Far, Farid Feyzi, Nima Esmi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Static Application Security Testing (SAST) tools play a critical role in identifying vulnerabilities during early software development stages; however, their effectiveness is often limited by extremely high false positive rates (FPRs). This paper introduces a hybrid workflow that integrates a high-recall static analyzer, Semgrep, with Large Language Model (LLM)–based validation to improve detection precision while maintaining high recall. Using the OWASP Benchmark v1.2 dataset comprising 2,740 labeled Java test cases, we systematically evaluate multiple models, including GPT-5-mini, GPT5-nano, and Claude-haiku, against a Semgrep-only baseline. Experimental results show that the proposed LLM-augmented static analysis drastically reduces the false positive rate from 91.8% to 11.9%, achieving a state-of-the-art F1-score of 92.84% and recovering 57% of vulnerabilities missed by Semgrep. Moreover, the entire validation process completes in under 44 minutes at a total cost of $1.44, confirming the feasibility of this approach for CI/CD integration. A detailed per-CWE and interaction analysis reveals that LLM validation eliminates 88.7% of Semgrep’s false alarms with minimal risk of overruling true positives (≈1%), while also exposing consistent weaknesses in cryptographic CWE-328 (Weak Hash). The results establish a practical pathway toward semantic, high-precision vulnerability detection through cost-efficient LLM validation layers.

title: Wind Curtailment Mitigation via Multi-Time-Scale Energy Storage System Scheduling Under Peak-Shaving Scenarios
authors: Jiayue Zhang, Xiaoyang Li, Qian Yang, Renjie Bao, Gejiaming Zhu, Yuanxing Xia
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper proposes a multi-time-scale coordination framework to quantify the standalone contribution of electrochemical energy storage to wind curtailment mitigation. The scheduling process consists of day-ahead, intra-day, and real-time stages, formulated as a scenario-based stochastic program with rolling forecast correction and solved using Gurobi via YALMIP–MATLAB. Compared with the no-EES case, wind curtailment is reduced from 0.50% to 0.21% under the positive peak-shaving scenario and from 5.32% to 4.81% under the negative peak-shaving scenario. Meanwhile, the mean absolute percentage error of net-load forecasting decreases from 0.57% in the day-ahead stage to 0.32% in the intra-day stage, and further to 0.23% in the real-time stage, demonstrating the effectiveness of the rolling updates. All numerical studies are fully reproducible using the open-source IEEE 30-bus test system.

title: Electric Field Distribution Characteristics of Composite Insulators under Salt Fog Environment
authors: Tianyu Xie, Minghui Sun, Haotian Wang, Yong Zhu, Kefeng Fu, Yuhan Xia, Liuyue Huang, Kunpeng Cheng
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This paper systematically investigates the surface electric field distribution characteristics of a post composite insulators widely used in coastal power systems under salt fog environments. Taking the clean insulator in dry air as the research baseline, a two-dimensional axisymmetric finite element model is established via COMSOL Multiphysics to quantitatively analyze the effects of salt fog conductivity, different dry band positions and lengths on the along-surface electric field. The results show that salt fog significantly enhances the peak electric field at the insulator ends and intensifies field distortion, with the potential gradient growth tending to saturate at high conductivity. Dry bands at all positions cause severe local field distortion, with the peak field always concentrated at both ends of the dry band and decreasing monotonically with increasing length. The findings provide a solid theoretical basis for anti-pollution flashover optimization design of composite insulators in salt fog areas.

title: Multi-Agent Game-Based Market Scheduling and Pricing of Primary and Secondary Frequency Reserve
authors: Tianyu Wang, Min Lu, Yuxiang Li, Jiangfeng Zhang, Hongyu Zhao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy weakens the frequency control capability of power systems and reduces frequency stability. To enhance system frequency stability under major disturbances, particularly by strengthening the capabilities of primary and secondary frequency reserves, it is essential to integrate multiple energy resources such as energy storage systems and demand response into the frequency regulation. Moreover, appropriate pricing is required to incentivize the coordinated participation of multiple energy resources. This paper extends the traditional economic dispatch model by incorporating dynamic constraints of primary and secondary frequency reserves provided by multiple energy resources. A marginal reserve price is employed to determine the pricing of primary and secondary reserves. Moreover, a multi-agent game framework is introduced to coordinate the strategic bidding competition of multiple energy resources, where each agent represents one energy resource type. Case studies on a 5-bus system demonstrate the proposed method improves frequency regulation capacity, total profits of market participants and overall market efficiency.

title: Optimal Dispatch of Rural Integrated Energy Systems Based on MOEA/D Algorithm
authors: Qiao Zhao, Ting Pan, Jianzan Yang, Dongjing Zou
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: In response to the policy requirements for green and low-carbon transformation of rural energy, and to address issues such as insufficient integration of renewable energy, high energy costs, and significant carbon emissions in rural Comprehensive Integrated Energy Systems (CIES), this paper conducts research on the optimal dispatch of CIES incorporating Integrated Demand Response (IDR). Firstly, a multi-energy complementary architecture incorporating biogas, wind, and solar power is constructed. Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS) and Power-to-Gas (P2G) technologies are integrated to achieve an internal carbon cycle within the system. Considering the characteristics of agricultural parks, an electrical-thermal-cooling IDR model that includes greenhouse environmental control loads is established. Secondly, a multi-objective optimization framework is formulated with the goals of minimizing economic cost, minimizing carbon emissions, and maximizing exergy efficiency. The Multi-objective Evolutionary Algorithm Based on Decomposition (MOEA/D) is employed to obtain a set of uniformly distributed Pareto non-dominated solutions. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed model is verified through a case study comparing four different scenarios. The results demonstrate that the proposed electrical-thermal-cooling IDR strategy reduces the total system cost by 5.93%, decreases carbon emissions by 10.53%, and improves exergy efficiency by 6.18%. Additionally, it flattens the peak-to-valley differences of electrical, cooling, and thermal loads by 33.56%, 23.64%, and 6.34% respectively, significantly enhancing the comprehensive benefits of the system.

title: A Knowledge-Enhanced Large Language Model Advisor for Degradation-Aware Microgrid Battery Dispatch Under Extreme Heat
authors: Sen Shen, Li Yi, Jun Wang, Jiaxu Duan, Yue Chen
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: Battery energy storage dispatch in microgrids involves a difficult tradeoff between operating cost and battery degradation, especially under extreme heat. This paper proposes a knowledge-enhanced large language model advisor for degradation-aware microgrid battery dispatch. The framework combines a knowledge graph (KG) of simulated operating cases, vector retrieval over battery degradation literature, and simulation-based validation to support grounded recommendation and explanation. Given an operator query, the system returns a control parameter recommendation together with causal interpretation and supporting evidence. Experiments reveal a nonlinear relationship between cost/lifespan and the cycling penalty weight. In a fresh 17 query closed-loop verification set, the proposed method achieves 100% consistency on in-distribution queries, 71.4% on out-of-distribution queries, and 88.2% overall. A separate 10-query ablation study further shows that the KG is the main driver of recommendation consistency, while vector retrieval mainly improves evidence grounding and traceable citation support.

title: A Data Transformation Method for TF Process Thickness Virtual Metrology System Optimization
authors: Chih-Chieh Chan, Wen-Hsiang Lin, Chia-Cheng Chang
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: Monitoring of wafers is a significant approach to maintaining inline process quality and equipment performance. All measurement-based wafer inspection requires enormous time and cost to be feasible, so Virtual metrology (VM) technology can fill the gap left by sampling inspection.In this study, we present a new transformation method to achieve a high correlation (0.62) between Virtual metrology prediction data and physical measurement data. Virtual metrology models are integrated with the Fault Detection and Classification (FDC) system to establish a real-time monitoring platform.

title: Research Trends in Cloud Computing-Based Signal Processing: A Bibliometric Analysis
authors: Kamyab Azizi, Fatemeh Aminizade, Elaheh Shafiei, Hasti Mirshams, Hossein Shahbodaghkhan, Mohammad Hossein Alizadeh Roknabadi, Farzaneh Mohammadi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Cloud computing now enables simultaneous, scalable, and cost-efficient signal analysis. To map progress in this interdisciplinary area, this study aims to conduct a comprehensive bibliometric analysis to explore research trends, collaboration networks, main contributors, and interdisciplinary and key research themes in cloud computing-based signal processing. A total of 5,269 published papers were extracted from the Web of Science database and analyzed using VOSviewer with specific thresholds for keyword co-occurrence and author collaboration. The study utilizes a quantitative bibliometric methodology to map the field's transition from traditional signal processing to AI-driven cloud architectures. The results show a growing trend in publications, as there has been a notable increase since 2010, with peaks in 2022 and 2024. Based on keyword co-occurrence analysis, core research themes are (i) remote sensing and atmospheric themes; (ii) algorithmic and learning-based processing; (iii) network and communication optimization; and (iv) IoT and data management. The results also reveal a varied distribution of contributors, including leading institutions such as the Jet Propulsion Laboratory and pioneering countries such as China and the United States. In addition, Electrical and Electronic Engineering is the key research category, and IEEE plays a main role in publishing papers in this field. Future research can be directed by considering the emerging trends revealed in this bibliometric analysis.

title: Lightweight Spam Detection with BOW and Deep Stacking
authors: Ahmad Azarnik, Arash Khosravi
publish: 2025 11th International Conference on Signal Processing and Intelligent Systems (ICSPIS)
abstract: Email remains the most widely used form of digital communication, yet the growing volume of spam messages continues to pose significant security and privacy threats. Existing research has predominantly relied on TF-IDF with classical machine learning algorithms or heavy deep learning architectures such as CNN, BiLSTM, and BERT. This study introduces a lightweight yet powerful alternative by combining Bag-of-Words (BoW) feature extraction with a stacked deep learning ensemble composed of Multi-Layer Perceptrons (MLPs) as base learners and a neural meta-learner. Unlike prior works, our approach demonstrates that simple word frequency features, when integrated through deep stacking, can surpass complex embedding-based and transformer models. Experiments on the Enron Email Spam Dataset show that our model achieves 98% accuracy, 99% precision, and 98% recall—comparable to techniques such as CNN and BERT, while being computationally more efficient This contribution highlights a novel balance between performance and efficiency, making the model highly suitable for scalable and real-world spam filtering applications.

title: A Study on the Pricing Regime for Integrated Hydro-Wind-Solar Energy Bases in River Basins within the Framework of Dual Carbon Objectives
authors: Sujie Qiao, Jiansheng Zhou, Shunli Jing, Jie Gao
publish: 2026 9th International Conference on Energy, Electrical and Power Engineering (CEEPE)
abstract: This study tackles pricing and benefit-sharing challenges in hydro-wind-solar energy bases under China's "Dual Carbon" goals. After reviewing current electricity pricing mechanisms, it identifies issues in existing schemes and proposes a unified feed-in tariff based on the "cost-plus-reasonable-return" principle. The recommendations aim to boost the competitiveness and coordinated development of integrated multi-energy systems.

title:
Impact of Precursor Temperature and Carrier Gas Flow Rates on Low-Temperature Atomic Layer Deposition of SiO
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Using BDEAS
authors: Bridgette Rodman, Cassidy Borden, Bas Korevaar
publish: 2026 37th Annual SEMI Advanced Semiconductor Manufacturing Conference (ASMC)
abstract: This paper studies the impact of precursor temperature and carrier gas flow rates on low-temperature atomic layer deposition process SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> using bis(diethylamino)silane (BDEAS) and O<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>-plasma as the oxidant. The deposition behavior of BDEAS under varying flow conditions—particularly in relation to carrier gas dynamics— remains underexplored. Experiments were conducted across Ar-carrier flow rates from 400 to 2000 sccm and precursor bottle temperatures from 50°C to 65°C, while maintaining all other process conditions constant. Film thickness (THK), growth per cycle (GPC), and wet etch rate (WER) were measured to assess the sensitivity of SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> deposition behavior to precursor delivery dynamics. Results show an inverse relationship between carrier gas flow and both THK and GPC, consistent with precursor dilution and reduced BDEAS partial pressure. Higher precursor temperatures increased vapor pressure and correspondingly elevated growth rates at all flow conditions. WER decreased with increased carrier gas flow, indicating a change in film characteristics under reduced precursor availability. These findings demonstrate the importance of balancing precursor temperature and carrier gas flow to maintain stable film growth, density, and etch characteristics—parameters essential for optimizing SiO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> hard mask deposition in advanced semiconductor manufacturing.

title: The Neuroscience of (Dis)Trust: Proactive Takeovers in Human–AV Interaction
authors: Yinsu Zhang, Ranjana K. Mehta, Anthony D. McDonald
publish: ACM Transactions on Human-Robot Interaction
abstract: None

title: Investigating Energy Bounds of Analog Compute-in-Memory with Local Normalization
authors: Brian Rojkov, Shubham Ranjan, Derek Wright, Manoj Sachdev
publish: IEEE Journal on Emerging and Selected Topics in Circuits and Systems
abstract: Modern edge AI workloads demand maximum energy efficiency, motivating the pursuit of analog Compute-in-Memory (CIM) architectures. Simultaneously, the popularity of large language models (LLMs) drives the adoption of low-bit floating-point formats that prioritize dynamic range. However, the conventional direct-accumulation CIM accommodates floating-point values by normalizing them to a shared, widened fixed-point scale. Consequently, hardware resolution is dictated by the input’s dynamic range rather than its precision, and energy consumption is dominated by the ADC. We address this limitation by introducing local normalization for each input, weight, and multiply-accumulate (MAC) output via a Gain-Ranging MAC (GR-MAC). Normalization overhead is handled by low-power digital logic, enabling the computationally expensive MAC operation to remain in the energy-efficient low-precision analog regime. Energy modelling shows that adding a gain-ranging stage to the MAC increases the input dynamic range by 4 bits without increasing energy consumption at a 35 dB SQNR standard. Additionally, the ADC resolution requirement becomes invariant to input distribution assumptions, allowing the construction of an upper bound with a 1.5-bit reduction compared to the conventional lower bound. These results establish a pathway to unlocking favourable energy scaling trends in analog CIM for modern AI workloads.

title: High-Frequency Motor Models for Capacitively and Inductively Coupled Bearing Voltages: Comparison of a Model With and Without Considering the Inductance for Zero Sequence Components
authors: Daan Deruyter, Fawzy A. Abdo, Karel Vanthuyne, Mehmet Güleç, Peter Sergeant
publish: IEEE Transactions on Energy Conversion
abstract: The common mode voltage present at the output of the inverter causes bearing voltages through a capacitive or an inductive coupling path, which in turn can result in damaging bearing currents. Accurate high-frequency motor models able to predict both of these coupling paths are required for a proper system design. This paper proposes a new high-frequency motor model for capacitively and inductively coupled bearing voltages. The model comes in two versions: the first model considers the difference in inductance experienced by zero sequence components compared to positive or negative sequence components while the second model neglects this difference. A hybrid method to fit the motor parameters from impedance measurements is proposed, and the resulting impedances are compared to measured impedances. The models are validated by comparing the simulated bearing voltage to measurements on two different setups: an inverter-cable-motor setup and a pulse generator setup. It is shown that including the difference in inductance for zero sequence components compared to positive or negative sequence components improves the impedance match at lower frequencies and the resonance frequency for common mode signals and allows a more accurate simulation of high-frequency oscillations in the bearing voltage. However, to include the difference in inductance an additional impedance measurement that requires access to the neutral point of the motor is required, which is not always possible.

title: Net-Zero Power UAVs: Facilitating Quantum HRLLC in Multi-Hop UAV Repeaters_supp1-3701590.mp4
authors: Ali Nawaz Ranjha
publish: N/A
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel joint optimization framework for autonomous, quantum-enabled UAV repeater networks designed to support hyper-reliable low-latency communications (HRLLC) in challenging environments such as post-explosion chemical plant operations. The proposed system uses a symmetric dual-UAV architecture with three sequential quantum channels operating under strict energy harvesting (EH) constraints. We formulate a highly challenging non-convex mixed-integer non-linear programming (MINLP) problem. This model jointly determines qubit allocation, 2-D uncrewed aerial vehicle (UAV) spatial positioning (distance and altitude), and transmit powers to minimize the total decoding error. Crucially, our framework explicitly models real-world quantum physics, including decoherence, gate imperfections, and entanglement degradation. Since the objective function is demonstrably non-convex, to efficiently solve the MINLP, we propose a quantum-resource-optimized GA (QRO-GA). The results of our numerical simulations show that the QRO-GA yields better results than benchmark methods and it is highly efficient, with the convergence speed significantly increasing with the total qubit allocation. Convergence is achieved in as few as 5 iterations for higher qubit allocation. Moreover, the results reveal a critical U-shaped tradeoff, proving that an optimal latency distribution within a strict 80 μs transmission window is strictly required to maintain HRLLC reliability. In addition, we find that the QRO-GA successfully optimizes the 2-D UAV positioning to minimize total decoding error. Finally, it balances consumed and harvested power to achieve net-zero power UAVs system.

title: Flow-Aware Synthetic Packet Generation with Header-Timing Dependency Modeling Using a Diffusion Model
authors: Yukito Onodera, Tomoya Kosugi, Takashi Nakanishi, Tatsuya Shimada
publish: IEEE Access
abstract: In recent years, analyses in the network and security domains have shifted from flow statistics-based approaches to more detailed analyses of packet-level traffic behavior, including packet header structures and inter-arrival time (IAT). These features are essential for capturing the internal structure and temporal characteristics of the many communication services. However, large-scale collection of real packet data in operational environments is difficult due to privacy concerns and operational costs. As a result, synthetic data generation techniques that preserve the statistical characteristics of real data are attracting increasing attention. Existing approaches either focus on generating tabular data based on flow statistics or fail to simultaneously reproduce both header structures and temporal dependencies when generating packet data. In this study, we propose a packet data generation method that jointly models the dependency between packet header structures and IAT. The proposed method models state transitions within a flow using difference-based learning and employs a conditional diffusion model incorporating FiLM and self-attention to enable the integrated generation of structural and temporal information. Experiments on the CICIDS-2017 dataset demonstrate that the proposed method improves the reproducibility of both packet header distributions and IAT distributions compared to existing methods. In an anomaly detection use case, models trained on the generated data achieved an AUC of 0.701, which is close to the 0.717 obtained when trained on real data, indicating that the proposed method successfully captures the dependency between packet header structures and temporal information.

title: Fabrication and Emission Performance of Nanoscandia-Diffusion Cathode via Mechanical Fusion Coating Method
authors: Zhiqian Yuan, Yan Li, Shixian Ding, Feng Ren, Xingqi Wang, Xiaoxia Wang, Xueliang He, Zenglin Zhou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Electron Devices
abstract: Vacuum microwave devices require cathode materials with high emission current density (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\ge$</tex-math> </inline-formula>50 A/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>) and long operational lifetime. To address issues of poor emission uniformity and limited reproducibility in Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>-W mixed-matrix cathodes, this study introduces a mechanical fusion coating process. Using classified tungsten powder (average size: <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3.8~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m; span <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\le 1.2$</tex-math> </inline-formula>), a uniform W@Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> structure nano-Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> coating (100–290nm) was applied. This method eliminated Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> agglomeration and yielded a sintered matrix with closed porosity <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\le 0.5$</tex-math> </inline-formula>%, a pore size of 0.6–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m, and carbon content <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\le 400$</tex-math> </inline-formula> ppm. At <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$1100~^{\circ }\text {C}_{\text {b}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>, the cathode achieved pulsed emission current densities of 149.86 A/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> (1% duty cycle) and 81.36 A/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> (5% duty cycle), representing a 108.1% improvement over planetary milled cathodes. The W@Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> increases specific surface area, supplying more active sites, while Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> migration to sintering necks enhances matrix stability. The uniform Sc<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> distribution ensures consistent sintering shrinkage, enabling batch-suitable cathodes with high structural stability and machinability.

title: Entropy-Embedded Partial Domain Adaption Network for Digital Twin-Enhanced Rolling Bearing Fault Diagnosis
authors: Xinyue Wang, Teng Wang, Yongchao Zhang, Qing Ni, Yongbo Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Reliability
abstract: Accurate and reliable bearing fault diagnosis is critical for ensuring the operational reliability of industrial machinery, yet it remains a challenge due to scarce labeled fault data and prevalent unknown health conditions. To enhance diagnostic reliability under such uncertainties, this paper proposes a digital twin (DT) augmented framework that integrates entropy-embedded partial domain adaptation. The approach constructs a high-fidelity virtual bearing model to reliably simulate fault dynamics and generate comprehensive fault signatures. By systematically applying entropy-based criteria to evaluate the uncertainty and complexity of features, the framework extracts and purifies the most informative and reliable characteristics between the physical and virtual domains. These purified features are aligned through a weighted block-diagonal structure, effectively mitigating domain shift caused by unknown health states and improving the reliability of knowledge transfer. This process ensures robust cross-domain diagnostics while minimizing negative interference from outlier conditions. Experimental validation across multiple bearing datasets confirms that the proposed framework reliably transfers diagnostic knowledge from virtual to physical systems, maintaining high fault identification accuracy even with completely unlabeled measurements. By leveraging entropy-based domain adaptation within a digital twin environment, this work achieves reliable fault diagnosis.
